If I could give negative stars I would! I haven’t even received the car yet but the customer service so far has been appalling. I ordered the Tesla model y as a lease vehicle in November through the Edinburgh dealership, making it clear I would need the car at the end of Jan when my current lease expires. They say no problem and they’ll make a note on the system as there are current cars in the country etc. however this did not happen! I started getting notifications the car was coming in December. When j finally got hold of someone they said it was an error and they would put a note for delivery to come in Jan. I was to let them know later when would be a good date. However when I contacted the beginning of Jan with the date my car was being collected, only then did they re-start the process of ordering my car!! So then I’ve been left waiting nearly 2 months, which cost me a lot to extend my current lease, buy new car insurance that I’ll need to cancel and be charged a cancellation fee and a service which I would not have had to pay for had the car gone back on time. Now if this wasn’t enough now I’ve received confirmation my car will be ready for delivery next week, but rather than it being delivered to me… they expect me to drive and hour and a half to collect it from Glasgow!! I’ve never had to do this with a lease car before and means that both me and my husband will have to take an afternoon off work to collect - and as we both work for ourselves this again has significant financial implications, not to mention the extra mileage I will no doubt be responsible for to get the car to my house! I queried why this couldn’t at least be delivered to Edinburgh, where I actually ordered it from! Never in the whole experience was I notified I would have to collect from Glasgow and if I was I would never have ordered the car! This will be my first and last Tesla, I have very low expectations of the car itself now but I will never order another even if it is ok. Be Warned! If you live in Scotland and not based in Glasgow or Aberdeen, I just wouldn’t bother

Massive disappointment. Systematic heat pump failure and €4,500 repair trap.I am deeply disappointed with Tesla Service Center Ribe. My 2022 Model Ysuffered a complete thermal system failure. This is a well-known, widespread issue with the 2022–2023 models’ heat pumps, especially failing during abnormally low temperatures.Despite this being a clear design flaw, Tesla refused to issue a recall and is now waiting for warranties to expire to charge customers for their own engineering mistakes. They quoted me which is nearly 10% of the car's total value! Instead of fixing the specific fault, they simply demand a total system replacement (Supermanifold and Compressor).I found other customers here with the exact same story: out-of-warranty cars, identical failure codes, and the same 30,000+ DKK price tag. It feels like a systematic way to shift the cost of a defective product onto loyal customers.To make matters worse, the car just failed its mandatory inspection (Syn). Not only is the heating system broken, but the brakes are rusted through—another common Tesla issue that they ignore. It is absurd that a car of this price and age cannot pass a basic safety check.Warning to potential buyers: Think twice before buying this "bucket of bolts." Tesla takes zero responsibility once the warranty hits 80,000 km. You are left with a massive financial liability.

Purchased a Tesla in late 2024 after a great FSD (Full Self-Driving) test drive where the car performed wonderfully. But once I received my own vehicle, I experienced multiple issues within weeks. On my third FSD use, the car failed to properly enter a left-turn lane and collided with another vehicle’s bumper. Multiple other attempts to use FSD left us disillusioned — the car makes poor driving decisions, and you need the reaction speed of Superman to stop it before something goes wrong.The windshield wipers didn’t function reliably until after a service appointment — despite the technician stating no repairs were made. Backup alerts are inconsistent, often missing both visual and audible warnings. In one case, I nearly backed into a moving car with no warning at all.I brought the car in for service and spent hours with the team, only to be told nothing was wrong. Ultimately, the service team said there was “nothing more they could do” and suggested submitting a buyback request — which I did. After more than 30 days and multiple follow-ups, I’ve received no response.We’ve stopped using FSD and installed a third-party screen to access continuous blind spot alerts, since Tesla’s native interface does not display them. Ultimately, I regret this purchase and do not feel Tesla took responsibility for the issues. I've given two stars to acknowledge that it is an electric car and has reduced our fuel expense — but it has increased our safety risks compared to the older vehicle it replaced.

Be careful if you want to lease a Tesla. usually lease means that you can exchange car for a new model, and of course you can buy the car back at the end of the lease. These terms are used by every car dealer I talked to. Therefore, when I saw different leasing options on Tesla, I selected a model and made a deposit.However, after payment, I received an email from Tesla with a request to proceed with the order. And here it turned out that Tesla's lease is completely different. After the end of the lease period you have to return the car. Exchange for a newer model is not an option at all.You will not find any mentioning of that anywhere on Tesla site while you are making an order. I’m posting this to warn other Tesla Model 3 owners about a deeply frustrating and expensive experience with my 2019 Model 3.Just 8 months out of warranty, my car completely shut down while driving — no warning, no accident, just a full power failure mid-ride. I had it towed to Tesla Service and was told the rear drive unit needed to be replaced, along with the pyrotechnic battery disconnect system. Total repair estimate? $4,832.13.What’s shocking is:•	This car has been well-maintained and has never been in a collision.•	The drive unit failure is a major issue in an EV — and feels like something Tesla should stand behind longer than the 4-year/50k mile warranty.•	Despite the seriousness of the failure, Tesla provided no warranty support or goodwill discount, aside from topping off windshield fluid and doing a tire pressure check for free.•	Labor was billed at $260/hour — higher than many luxury dealerships.•	No recall, no service bulletin, no offer of assistance. Just a huge bill.I get that cars cost money, and EVs have complex components. But a $2,500+ drivetrain failure this soon after warranty expiry? That’s unacceptable. Tesla should at minimum have offered partial coverage for something so critical — especially when it involves safety systems like the pyrotechnic disconnect.If you own or are considering a Tesla out of warranty, be prepared: you’re one breakdown away from a massive repair bill, and there’s no safety net once you cross that warranty line.Ask about extended warranty plans, and be cautious of the “low maintenance” pitch. My experience proves that when something breaks, it breaks big — and Tesla leaves you on the hook.

I bought a Tesla Model S two years ago.My Model S was great to drive and had more performance than I had ever had in a car. It was comfortable for long journeys across Europe and their supercharger network is a 'killer app' that enabled me to travel long distances with not too much planning.They are let down by poor quality - I had to get three significant paint blemishes fixed (free by Tesla) after delivery and the interior materials and quality are not a patch on luxury German cars.Sadly, they couldn't sort out a problem with the media chip on the car: Sometimes, after about a year, I had no media sound for hours, bluetooth wouldn't initialise and I had to learn to perform reboots on the whole system from time to time, sometimes while driving. I also lost confidence after the car's autopilot steered me suddenly into a kerb, damaging a wheel. I mentioned it to Tesla staff, but they didn't seem concerned.I decided to sell the car and asked Tesla for a trade-in price. Their offer was less than 50% of the sales price - so a huge depreciation. I sold it privately in a week for just under 70% of the purchase price. That suggests to me that Tesla are giving no support to the second hand market, but are prioritising massive profit from trade-ins.I loved the car despite its faults and am very likely to buy another one: There have been quite a few updates over the last two years and access to the Tesla supercharger network rules out any other electric cars for me at the moment.I do also have a Powerwall and that is performing way above my expectations after about a year in service. Customer service and information over the phone and email has been way better for me than email and phone service for the car, though the car service centre staff have always been really helpful and polite.
Tesla is great but I don’t own a Tesla, now if I did this would definitely be a 5 star review!Amazing what they’re doing

My experience .. I've had my S 75 RWD since Dec 2017. I've driven over 26k relatively trouble free miles including a recent 2k round trip to the Italian Alps & back last Winter. Tesla's coupe d'etat is their supercharging network, it is second to none & I didn't need do any trip planning beyond a distance map on A Better Route Planner to work out where to book overnight & expect meal stops. The in-car navigation system handled getting us from door-to-door admirably. When permitted, we also drive all over the UK and often go on impromptu day trips which we would never have done in my old car due to cost of petrol. We use the supercharger network whenever we do this, we've never had to use another public charging system, & there is zero inconvenience .. we simply stop for food as we would have to do anyway & plug the car in. The car is usually ready to go before we are! In general day-to-day use we charge at home from solar power where we can or low cost overnight electricity from a renewable supplier where we can't. I can even top-up, for free, when I go shopping as Tesco has multiple free-to-use PodPoint chargers.My car is a joy to drive. I have the performance of a BMW M3 (mine is a "slow" Tesla), Spotify on tap for music, TuneIn for podcasts, a HEPA filter to deal with city pollution, funky country smells & hayfever then AutoPilot to top it all and take the strain on long motorway journeys. All the while cruising along in serene silence emitting zero CO2. I have an app to allow me to heat or cool my car remotely, to let someone into my car & drive it if I need them to even if I'm in another part of the world & I receive regular software updates that add features that my car didn't have when I bought it (a built in dashcam, a video alarm system, "dog mode" to allow animals/people/cream cakes to stay in the car on hot/cold days and maintain the temperature, a multitude of in-car games).I have taken my car to a local HEVRA garage to have it checked over for roadworthy-ness twice, Tesla do not mandate servicing but I feel an annual mechanical check-up is wise. I've not had to change my wiper blades, tyres, brake pads or brake discs (despite having attended 2 separate runway days to safely see just how fast my car will go (140mph) and to test the handling on very wiggly sprint circuits); I've had the HEPA filter cleaned and the pollen filter changed; I have had to top up my screen wash several times .. the costs have been minimal.I've had some warranty claims which have usually been resolved quickly and with minimal fuss, usually by a mobile technician at a place of my choosing, work or home. Issues have been: some cabin rattles/squeaks (you wouldn't have noticed these in a petrol/diesel vehicle due to engine noise), some wind noise (again, would not have been noticeable in a none electric vehicle), a failed bonnet solenoid (manual unlatching was still possible), a steering rack squeak (more grease required) and a seat pad which mysteriously discoloured.I've deducted 1 star as there is always room for improvement & customer communication from Tesla could be better. Because of this they tend to seem a little disorganised when it comes to vehicle collection/service even if, in actual fact, things as far as they're concerned, are all good.So, I would recommend Tesla but with the caveat of they are a tech company making electric vehicles, not a car company trying to do an EV. Tesla will sell you a car in the same way that Apple will sell you a phone .. don't expect them to flatter your ego on the way. If you like buttons & traditional cabins with dealers that fawn over you whilst trying to get an up-sell of some sort, then they won't be for you. If you are a bit of a tech geek willing to accept a bit of rough-round-the-edges while they get it right, well, you won't find a faster "milk float" ;)

I love my cyber truck.I wasn’t sure what to expect when I picked up the Cybertruck.  I've now had it for over a year.  It's the best vehicle I've ever owned.  People get too caught up in politics, EV polarization, or simply the fact that its different than what they're used to.The Cybertruck delivers on the hype. It’s absurdly powerful, eerily quiet, and built like a better tank. The steel exterior turns heads everywhere—and despite the rugged look, it rides incredibly smooth.The interior is deceptively spacious and thoughtfully designed. The tech is intuitive (as you’d expect from Tesla), and the turning radius is surprisingly tight for a truck this size. Off-road performance? Absolutely bonkers. I’ve already taken it across terrain I wouldn’t have dared with other 4x4s.What really sets it apart is how fun it is to drive. It feels like a tank and a spaceship had a baby—and it hauls like one too.Yes, it’s unconventional. No, it’s not for everyone, but it should be. For me? Its hands down the most exciting, capable, and futuristic vehicle I’ve ever owned.

I had an unforgettable experience at the Tesla Sheffield store, where the team, led by the phenomenal Paige and her colleagues, went above and beyond by offering me an overnight test drive of the Tesla Model Y Launch Edition. Their professionalism and enthusiasm made the entire process seamless, showcasing the car’s brilliance in a way that left me truly impressed.Having tested other electric vehicles like the Volkswagen ID.4 and BMW iX, I can confidently say the Tesla Model Y is light-years ahead. Those competitors’ reliance on Apple CarPlay was a nightmare—laggy, unintuitive, and frustratingly unresponsive, with small screens that made app control a chore. Charging them was equally cumbersome, requiring multiple apps and lacking clear navigation for charging stops. In stark contrast, the Model Y’s massive, crystal-clear, lag-free touchscreen is a game-changer, offering seamless phone integration and an intuitive interface that’s a joy to use.Planning trips is effortless, with the navigation system clearly mapping out Supercharger stops, making charging fast and stress-free. After this drive, it’s clear that internal combustion engines are relics of the past—Tesla has redefined what driving can be.Compared to earlier Model Y versions, the Launch Edition’s suspension is a revelation, difference like night and day, while road noise is virtually eliminated, creating a serene cabin. The upgraded seats are exceptionally comfortable, and the ventilated front seats were a lifesaver on a sweltering 28-29°C day, keeping me cool and sweat-free.The reclining rear seats, paired with a dedicated touchscreen for passengers to control music, videos, and climate, add a luxurious touch that my family loved. With generous legroom for tall passengers and easy installation of a baby seat, the Model Y is a dream for families.The panoramic glass roof, infused with silver inserts to block UV rays and heat, kept the cabin comfortable and stunningly bright, even in blazing sun.The Model Y’s luggage compartment is a standout, far surpassing competitors like the Volkswagen ID. While the ID forced me to puzzle bags and adjust seats to fit them, the Model Y’s spacious trunk effortlessly swallowed four massive suitcases with zero hassle—packing was completely stress-free.The single stalk for turn signals is a thoughtful feature, especially for those not used to the button-based system I tried (and loved) in the new Model 3—though I opted for the Model Y’s family-friendly space. The ambient lighting, with infinite color options, creates a stylish, personalized vibe, and the audio system is phenomenal—crisp, immersive, and far superior to my old BMW 6 Series’ Harman Kardon setup.This test drive was nothing short of transformative.The Model Y’s blend of cutting-edge technology, unmatched comfort, and family-oriented practicality, combined with the UK’s unmissable 0% APR offer, sealed the deal for me to place an order. A heartfelt thank you to Paige and the Tesla Sheffield team for an exceptional experience—I’m beyond thrilled to join the Tesla family and can’t wait to hit the road in my Model Y!

At this stage, communication was good and I had no complaints.The car went back to BYD on 14 October and remained with them for two months. I was later told the smoke was caused by overfilled oil from a previous service carried out by BYD themselves, and that draining the excess oil would resolve the issue.When I finally collected the car, several agreed repairs had not been completed. At this point, BYD stated they would do no further work and instead offered a £850 refund, which did not cover the outstanding repairs. This refund was not related to the smoke issue, but for items such as:•	Chipped windscreen•	Faulty centre armrest•	Front bumper clipsI accepted the refund simply to get my car back.Five weeks later, the smoking problem has not stopped and has actually worsened. I contacted BYD again, only to be told they would no longer help and that I must rely on the warranty, and if the warranty does not pay out, I would be responsible for the costs myself.Independent mechanics have advised this could be a head gasket, valve stem seals, or turbo issue — all extremely expensive repairs.I have now been forced to contact the finance company, raise a case with the Motor Ombudsman, and seek legal advice. I am potentially facing a loss of £18,000 on my first proper car — the exact situation I was trying to avoid by buying from a main dealership.Once BYD had my money, their attitude completely changed. This has been a terrible experience with their used car sales, and I would strongly advise anyone considering buying a second-hand vehicle from BYD Royston to stay well clear. I cannot comment on their new cars, but their after-sales support for used vehicles is unacceptable.

The BYD Seal U Hybrid is an impressive all-rounder that combines modern design, practicality, and cutting-edge technology. It feels like a solid, well-built car with a smooth and quiet ride — perfect for both city driving and longer journeys.The battery performance is excellent, offering great efficiency and reliability, while the hybrid system delivers seamless transitions between electric and petrol power. It’s also incredibly easy to drive, with responsive handling and a comfortable, refined feel on the road.Inside, the Seal U is packed with gadgets and thoughtful features. The 360-degree camera makes parking effortless, and the automatic speed alert helps you stay within limits safely. The electric seat adjustments add to the comfort, and the self-closing boot at the touch of a button is a real convenience. Another handy feature is the car locator, which makes finding your vehicle in a big car park quick and stress-free.Overall, the BYD Seal U Hybrid is a feature-rich, comfortable, and efficient car that offers exceptional value for money. It’s a great choice for anyone looking for a modern hybrid packed with tech and comfort, and value for money. Much cheaper than a Tesla!

Before I start, I hate that I am even having to do this, I have had two Polestars and I love the cars, however, it's the service I received after getting my car that I now have my issue with. Folks take note!! I purchased my lovely new DM PS4, I live in Northern Ireland and due to there being no main Polestar dealership here, I had to go to Glasgow to pick it up. I collected the car on the 26th Sept 25. The dealership is in a shopping mall and the guy who was maning the shop was very pleasant and helpful, but I had to wait for over an hour, well past my appointment time, for the manager to arrive with my car. Eventually he did and we went out to see the car. At handover, Ross the manager, showed me around. He then informed me that the key card, the 2nd key to my car wasn't working. He said that it wouldn't program and he would have to send me out a new key card the following week. Well I waited, and waited, weeks turned to months and on and on with no sign of my new spare key. I called many times to PS Glasgow, I was assured many many times that Ross would call me back, and to this day, I still have not received a single call back. The shop guy has fobbed me off so many times now, I think he dreads me calling. I then tried my luck with Polestar head office, who again promised the world and delivered nothing. Eventually in mid Jan my wife also bought an EV and for the purposes of car movement and charging, I now really needed  to give her my spare key when I was not home. I called the local Lookers dealer in Glasgow and found a very helpful lady, who looked up my details on the system. No new key had ever been ordered and there was nothing on record. She order the key and sent it over to me, now Feb 2025, 5 months later, I was given instruction to take it to Belfast Volvo and they would program it for me under warranty. Thinking this would now be the end of my key saga, oh no! They advised me there would be a £153 charge to program the key. This would have been completed under warranty if Ross had of given me the broken key at the time. I then called Lookers, who patched me through to PS Glasgow, again... I got put through to a lad called Ben. Ben was very apologetic and asked me to send an email with the receipt and invoice and an explanation of what happened. He assured me that it would be followed up and that a swift resolution would happen. He promised me the manager from Glasgow PS would call me, I held hope, 1 last time, but unfortunately, Ross still never called me. 2 weeks later, still no call, no refund, and no apology for their terrible after service. For as much as I love the car and I really do,  the aftercare from PS Glasgow is not only deeply disappointing and an embarrassment to the brand, it's actually  non existent! I have now unfortunately had to send a complaint to PS head office, dear knows how long I will wait for any response, and to top it all off, I am still £153 out of pocket because, our manager friend from PS Glasgow, couldn't manage a teddy bears picnic party for 1. I guess buying a 73k car from Polestar doesn't have the same depth of value for its customers as the likes of Audi or BMW have for their customers, who in my experience, move mountains to fix this sort of stuff. Such a pity that we are here, but take note Polestar buyers, make sure you get all your keys, because waiting for any sort of aftercare is simply not there, and you end up going in circles. Sad times we live in! 😔😟🙁

Very disappointing aftersales support.My Polestar 3 suffered an onboard charger failure and was recovered on 19 February 2026. It was diagnosed on 20 February 2026, and I was then told the required part was on back order with no ETA.Polestar’s proposed solution has been that I collect the car unrepaired and rely on public DC charging, with charging costs reimbursed. I leased a Polestar 3 in April 2025 on a Business Contract Hire through Lex Autolease. In under 12 months I have experienced three serious failures resulting in over 60 days without the vehicle.Failure 1 — Safety Recall: The car was subject to a manufacturer safety recall requiring workshop repairs. 10 days without the vehicle.Failure 2 — Complete Key Fob System Failure: Every key, fob and card simultaneously failed, leaving the car sealed shut and completely inaccessible from inside or outside. This happened immediately before a planned family holiday, which was severely disrupted. 14 days without the vehicle.Failure 3 — Catastrophic Steering Failure: At just 5,000 miles, the steering system completely failed while I was driving. The vehicle required emergency recovery and has been diagnosed as needing a complete steering rack replacement. I have reported this to DVSA as a Category 1 safety-critical defect. The vehicle was collected on 17th January 2026 — as of today, 23rd February, I still do not have it back. That is 37 days for this single repair alone.Additionally, I experienced a serious GDPR security breach where a previous loan vehicle remained active in my app for 23 days, allowing me to unlock, lock, and track the location of another customer's car.Throughout all of this, communication from Polestar has been non-existent unless I chase. I have sent over 97 emails. Every update has had to be initiated by me. Promises to call back are routinely broken. My formal complaint sent in February 2026 has received no substantive response.I also want to highlight that Lex Autolease, as the leasing provider, have been equally unhelpful throughout this process. Despite being the finance provider and therefore jointly responsible under the Consumer Rights Act 2015, they have failed to meaningfully intervene or support resolution of any of these issues.I have filed a formal complaint with The Motor Ombudsman and reported the steering defect to DVSA.If you are considering a Polestar 3, please research this thoroughly before committing. A steering rack failure at 5,000 miles on a near-new vehicle is not normal, and the service support when things go wrong is wholly inadequate for a premium brand.
The Polestar app and software are shocking, fragile and poor.

Before we purchased the vehicle in November 2024, we contacted Polestar about an extended warranty and were told it was not yet available but would be soon. With seven months of standard warranty remaining (until June 2025), we were reassured that this would be sufficient time for Polestar to introduce an option. I contacted Polestar monthly for updates and was eventually told in May 2025 to register my interest by email, which I did. I was assured I would be contacted as soon as the extended warranty became available. I continue to check regularly, but no information has ever been provided. We had a problem in April 2025 and the repair was paid in full.On 5 January 2026, a second major issue occurred: the heating system failed, and the car would only charge slowly on a DC charger. My husband, who has fibromyalgia, uses the car daily, and sitting in –11°C temperatures for an hour just to reach 70% charge had a significant impact on his health. We waited a week for a booking, and we are now facing a £4,700 repair bill.We were told by Volvo that no courtesy car could be provided because the required part is on back order until at least February, with no confirmed date. Polestar did offer a courtesy car at one point, but it never arrived, this was then cancelled without our knowledge. After hours of phone calls and contradictory emails—some suggesting the repair might be fully covered—we were then informed that we must pay 50% of the bill because £2,500 had been deducted for a courtesy car that we never actually received. The car has now been off the road for over three weeks. I have spent 11.5 hours on the phone trying to get clarity. We have owned the car for only 16 months and driven 15,000 miles; the vehicle itself has just over 25,000 miles on it.I was travelling 100 miles a day to get my husband to and from work, we had no choice but to purchase a £500 diesel runaround. After tax, insurance, MOT, and four new tyres, we are already close to £1,000 out of pocket—and it costs £58 a week to run. Going from a premium electric vehicle to a £500 stop‑gap car is frustrating, embarrassing, and only fuels the anti‑EV narrative. Meanwhile, we are still waiting to hear what Polestar will cover under goodwill. Promised call‑backs do not happen, and each new email seems to contradict the last. We bought this car in good faith and were repeatedly assured that an extended warranty would be available. Had we known otherwise, we would NOT have purchased it. Had the extended warranty been available as promised, I believe we would have had a courtesy car and the repair covered in full.At this point, I do not know where else to turn. I am asking whether Polestar can offer a buy‑back or provide another form of meaningful resolution.Any assistance or direction you can provide would be greatly appreciated. If there is someone within Polestar who can help resolve this situation, I would be grateful if you could pass on my details

I was told we were unable to cancel the order and that once it was delivered to contact support to arrange a return. I have been contact support for months, and no one gets back to me. Every call I make I am told a note will be put on file and that the issue will be escalated, but again, I hear nothing back. It's been over 4 months now.

Worst car company I have ever dealt withHad a polestar 2 for 3 years the car I liked however if there was a problem forget trying to call polestar supportThe car turned up 3 months late leaving me having to get another car in between and was just as bad when trying to get rid of it, number of calls to the finance company and a number of days income lost trying to sort it out. Trying to raise a complaint and can take upto 8 weeks and asking for evidence which was all phone calls which obviously I don’t record phone calls.AVOID

The reserved Polestar 4 that never was...I had been looking at P4's for some months and arranged a test drive through Sheffield Branch.4 days prior to that I saw on car I was interested in on Polestar website, it was a high spec car delivery Jan-26 that worked well for me. I started to the process of reserving the car through their portal and had 5 days in which to pay £500 deposit.The test drive went, the sales guy from Polestar was great and answered all my questions, I had some comparison figures for Polestar Leasing and another Leasing company that I ran past Polestar trying to get a better price. There was no flexibility on Polestar side which was fine.Finally decided to go ahead and pay my deposit before my reservation time expired. Only to find the car that was supposedly allocated to me was now not available and had been "sold to another customer".Im trying to understand how and why that happened and have contacted Polestar for a response.Looking at some of the comments on customer service here, just wondering if I have just dodged a bullet?.I wasn't sure how few stars to rate my experience with Polestar, as I have not yet been an actual customer..Although wanting to be.Lets see what customer service come back with, I will update this chat.

Great cars let down by spalling customer service.  It’s almost as if they don’t want to sell cars.  Poor locations and using dealerships that sell other vehicles which explains the very lack lustre pre sale experience.Have been trying to get access to test drive a polestar 3 but nearest dealership is well over an hour away and I am in the county town of Essex.Polestar could definitely learn a thing or two about service from Tesla and they should really work on the brand identity.  Trying to say they are separate from Volvo but still relying on Volvo for servicing but not allowing Volvo to stock the cars to make them more accessible for potential customers.It is a real shame because having driven the Polestar 4 it’s it’s fantastic - if only the rest of the brand lived up to the product
Many cases reported of the on board ac charger failing. Going nearly 4 months without a fix Indifferent customer service. Truly awful experience.

Had a brand new Polestar 3 delivered on 7th August. The car was supposed to be delivered on the 6th, but the delivery driver returned the car, as it had numerous errors being displayed. When the car arrived it had multiple scratches on the door plastic trim and the frunk catch release area. Over the next couple of days the car started displaying more errors, dropped the mobile data connection without warning, refused to recognise the digital key, wouldn’t unlock or lock, the main driver display started going blank and on an unlit road the lights died without warning. Polestar dispatched a roadside technician and he couldn’t diagnose the car as hundreds of faults were being generated every minute, he eventually managed to get the car towed to a Volvo dealer. The car is totally unreliable and I’m very close to rejecting it. The polestar support centre just regurgitated items off a list they didn’t have a clue about what they were doing. I had resort to pushing the contact button in the car to get in contact with anyone that had a clue on what to do. I seriously doubt that a proper PDI check and final testing were conducted on the vehicle. The cars are great when they work,but that isn’t for long!I’ve replied to Polestars email detailing all the current issues. I’m giving you one attempt to resolve all these problems. If the car returns with the same or new problems, it will be rejected.Roadside assistance looked at car yesterday. 100’s of faults were being generated every minute. They took the car to Volvo dealership, they extracted four pages of unique errors but have no idea what’s causing them or how to fix it. At that point I contacted the lease company and rejected the car.It’s unroadworthy and too dangerous to drive. The car should never have been delivered in this condition

I had a Polestar 2 and absolutely loved it - I've now upgraded to a Polestar 4 which in my opinion is definitely worth it - if they could get the internet to work. Unfortunately there was an issue with my Polestar accounts and they have 2 accounts set up for me, with my car linked to one and the internet linked to the other. It's been 4 months of them continuously telling me the accounts have now been merged, but they never are. And every time I get in touch, they can see the issue.I've asked for it to be escalated, I've asked for a complaint to be raised, but it never is and the guy looking after the issue is almost always on extended leave - my request for it to be moved to someone else doesn't seem to be getting picked up either. I would assume when you have staff on extended leave, you have a colleague cover their work.It's just so disappointing. We're road tripping in a week's time and still have no internet which is a very very big pain. Especially when the car costs so much every month. I feel quite ripped off at this point.

I've now owned my new polestar 3 for for around 2 months, The car is good to drive and feels well built (historically i've driven either BMW or Merc for the last 20 years) and the car is certainly as well put together, however the ongoing 'software' issues are becoming very wearing. So far on multiple occasions either my wife or I have experienced the following:One pedal driving turning itself whilst drivingScreen behind the steering wheel turns offLarge screen looks like a bar codeRegular and various warning lights for sensors not workingPhone not connecting effectively when getting in the carCar wont lock with digital keyCar wont open with digital keyCar wont lock with card keyCar wont open with car keyThe app rarely reflects the actual charge in the carIn fact the app seems pretty much useless as the open / close from the app rarely worksIt's actually becoming a bit of a joke, with us often wondering "I wonder what will be the issue today"The technology really is incredibly poor and at best very inconsistent, with the vehicle costing nearly 80k is really is very disappointing to say the least.On the first day of ownership, I rang Polestar Service as a senor warning appeared, I was told 'don't worry it will just be a software issue, it'll sort itself out so there is no need to book it in' however after 2 months of 'niggles' some more frustrating / dangerous than others it's fair to say I wont be purchasing a Polestar again.... ever!!

I have recently cancelled an order for a used, (pre registered), Polestar2. I'm a mature customer and it would have been a private cash sale, probably my last car purchase. Having found exactly the vehicle I was looking for, the sales experience made me feel very uncomfortable. I don't mind buying certain products via the internet, but to spend that amount of money in the way Polestar wishes just didn't feel right. I'm not a fleet manager spending someone else's money. The process I went through didn't involve any member of the Polestar sales team on a face to face basis. It was done at a Volvo dealership with a person who openly accepted he knew nothing about the vehicle brand. Credit to him for going away to find answers to questions I asked. I felt as though I had no one to turn to should something go wrong (after reading owner comments and press reviews of the brand), Polestar is a high risk purchase. Take it to any Volvo dealership being the answer, but then, they are simply acting as a front for the company and reading that Volvo has reduced its involvement with Polestar, the future was not comforting to me. Yes, information on the 'net can be misleading, but when I looked at paperwork and at what was on their web site, there are inconsistencies. I rang Polestar customer service for a question about their current, (no pun intended), home car charger promotion. After a 30 minute wait I spoke with a polite person who directed me to the company they working with to answer my question, Polestar didn't know the answer about installation parameters involved to get  what was free installation of an item that could be bought cheaper elsewhere. When I called to cancel the order the next day, I got straight through to the same member of staff who remembered me and asked me for the answer to my previous question. No problems in cancelling my order, but even their cancellation terms on paperwork sent to me and what is on their web site is different. No consistency. Sorry, this is not the way to sell vehicles, take it or leave it, I chose the second option. They sent me a survey form and I gave the same rating. If Polestar wants to sell their vehicles through a dealership, Volvo or whatever, then get their dedicated staff in the building and have their own section on the site.

I purchased a Polestar 2 new in September 2022. As a driving machine, it’s genuinely impressive - well-balanced, responsive, and surprisingly engaging for an EV. Credit is due to the engineers who tuned its dynamics so well.Unfortunately, that excellent engineering is badly let down by the software experience. I've faced persistent issues: frequent glitches, a laggy screen, Google Maps taking up to three minutes to become usable, random screen reboots while driving, and even temporary losses of heating and audio. Most recently, my car has developed a TCAM fault that's rendered core features inoperable. I’ve lost all app connectivity, I can no longer adjust the battery’s maximum charge level (stuck at 39%), and the car is reporting 0 max amps for charging.I attempted to get support through Polestar's chat system - twice the session was terminated after a long wait. I then waited 45 minutes on the phone (starting as 20th in the queue), only to be told I needed to book in with a Volvo dealer. I called Arnold Clark Stirling Volvo and was promised a call back. After an hour with no response, I visited in person only to be told they can’t even look at the car for a month.This ownership experience has been utterly soul-destroying. I’ve owned several Lexus vehicles in the past, and even on the rare occasions they needed dealer visits, the process was smooth, prompt, and professional - an entirely different standard.I had been seriously considering the Polestar 4 as a next car. But after this experience, I simply cannot trust the brand. Sadly, this will be my first and last Polestar.

Bought a year old Polestar 2 in Feb 2025. There was a fault after 5 weeks. The after care, was shocking. Took forever to get through online and phone, we did all the chasing to find out what was happening with our car. No local service centre would take our car to be repaired, so it was set miles away. When it was fixed, they said we should have to collect it. Non of this was our fault! I’ve requested a manager to call me two days ago and currently still waiting. I’ve also sent an email to the aftercare team and have yet to receive a response.

I’ve been a Polestar owner for more than 4 years and have just ordered another one. As you might gather, I’ve been impressed.   This latest one is an “available car” and due for delivery next month.  Except….. apparently it’s still being built in China.  Was I told?  Of course not.  I’m only the customer.  From my perspective, this says everything about the discrepancy between Polestar the car and Polestar “support”.  If people ever conclude that “support” and car are the same thing, the company could be in an even worse financial position.  The staff at the customer edge of Polestar Support are well meaning, but probably underpaid and certainly undertrained.  I would like to see some of the unnamed managers step forward and reveal themselves.  It’s all a great pity.Update on 24th March.  I’m pleased to have received an acknowledgment from Polestar but still feel that all of this could have been avoided by better communications.And here we go again (23 May). Practically impossible to make contact or obtain satisfactory answers.  New car to be delivered in just over 2 weeks.  Need to obtain insurance as current insurer (Aviva) no longer covers Polestar.  Should I be worried?  Want to transfer cherished number plate and have submitted documentation to Polestar.  Don’t know if they’ll do it because they claim the car is “remarketed”, despite the fact it was still being built when I placed the order in March.   Told by Polestar Support that potential insurers will accept the VIN if registration not known.  Untrue.  Even Polestar’s own insurer won’t do that.  Can’t talk to registration team because they’re not customer-facing. Or some such jargon.  Do Polestar have any idea about what constitutes effective communication?

Polestar 2 (LRDM 2022), Very safe, fun and stable to drive. Build quality pretty good. Performance is fantastic. Software is terrible, extremely glitchy. 6 months left on 3 year lease. TCAM failure, propulsion failure, head lamp system failure. Constantly doing system resets, demanded and not demanded. SIM card resets. DAB radio intermittently connects. When data connection becomes unreliable or slow the car seems to hang and glitch. Reversing camera slow to initiate. Much more. Can't wait to give it back, lovely car but really frustrating to live with.

Great car, buggy software, inconsistent hardware, poor support.  I am 3 weeks in to owning a new P2 and am starting to feel more stressed about using it day to day than any other car I have ever owned.Take a look at the forums.  Keys not working, app which is less than functional, dead batteries etc etc.  These things happen but surely the dev team can get it so if I click on Radio that I get the radio!Am sat here on hold as a promised recovery vehicle still has not turned up.  Exhausting.If you can get over the Musk thing, the Tesla offers a much more polished experience.  Shame they dont have the quality of the Polestar ride.

Bought a top-spec Polestar 2 the moment they were available in Australia for $105k.I have had a recurring suspension issue (which ironically only affects the more premium suspension package) for more than a year. Polestar (via their Volvo service partner) have tried 3 times to repair the car, the most recent time promising that a new part had been developed just for this issue.I waited 6 months for this new part. The service report says the new part has been fitted, after discussion with Polestar on the phone it appears the same dud old part has been fitted to my car, and the issue remains. Did they even test-drive the car after the 'repair'?!At what point am I entitled to a buy-back? They won't commit to anything in writing, the time to get back to you about anything is measured in weeks rather than days, the customer service sound like they're reading an empathy script and it has all spoiled what is an otherwise good car.Now they pinky promise they'll actually fit the new part for real this time. At best incompetent, at worst - crooks. Do yourself a favour, do some reading on their financial outlook before buying a lemon you might be stuck with when they go into voluntary administration.
Wish customer service was more responsive

I have a 23 plate polestar 2 as my company car….Car has the most pointless centre consul.  One major advantage of EVs is the interior space it crates, but the PS2 has this ugly, wide centre consul, the edges literally cut into your legs, reducing space and reducing flexibility.  The phone ‘key’ is plagued with bugs and always fails to recognise the phone, every single day.  It isn’t the phone (it’s been changed and is a very new model) it isn’t the OS (it’s been changed and is latest) it’s the car.  Sometimes we have to stand outside the car for up to a minute just trying to get the car to recognise the phone.  If it’s raining, we’re getting very wet!!  Then when inside the car, it often doesn’t recognise the phone again, so you wave it around inside the car like there’s something wrong with you, and eventually it decides it can see the phone after all.The infotainment does weird things all the time; volume disappears, loses connection, unresponsive, indicators stop working etc.  The screen responsiveness isn’t great, and responsiveness for maps absolutely terrible.  The choice of apps is quite restricted, there is no ‘fun’ like Teslas offerings.  The screen being vertical does not lend itself to video use.  There is no phone mounting point really, and no wireless charging offering.The front usb sockets are in a stupid place, leaving you no choice but to have cables poking out and on view.  The seats have electric controls apart from seat angle… prob one of the most needed ones, and the handles so tight to the side, REALLY awkward to adjust.  The boot ‘wave your foot under’ function doesn’t work with a phone.  The glove compartment is tiny.  Boot space too small for a car of this class; I mean actual practical width and depth.  Can’t even fit a golf bag in properly.In trying to squeeze it in once my hand brushed past the inner plastic covering the rear liner, a sharp edge tore a 2” long piece of skin off my hand.Car has been back to Volvo (because no polestar dealers) twice.  Both times they were absolutely useless, doing nothing but resetting the system, something we did ourselves and we told them both times if that is what they will do then no point taking the car in.  I took extra time to describe all the issues in great detail… they clearly didnt even read it.I’ve had the car completely shut down on me when returning to it when miles away form home, and had to call the AA, only for the car to wake up again and hour later.  Embarrassing and quite a problem and something widely reported.  Imagine if this was in the middle of nowhere and / or I was more vulnerable person.It’s by a clear mile the worst car and worse service I’ve ever had.  Unfortunately it was my company car choice so I have to keep it for 3 years.  Can’t wait for it to end.  Will never touch one again, absolute pile of poo.I had a Tesla 3 (one of the first ones released) and even that being an ‘immature’ vehicle at the time, was head and shoulders ten times the car the polestar is.  I can’t find the words to describe how poor and regrettable the whole Polestar experience has been.

I've owned a P*3 since October and LOVE most things about the car.  Hopefully the CPU upgrade and subsequent software updates will resolve some annoying little issues, and add some missing functions.  This may be price to be paid for trusting leading-edge technology!I would raise one issue with the Customer Experience Team.I had a complete fail on a trip to France requiring shipping the car to the nearest Volvo dealer for analysis and repair.Case Management was by Polestar Support UK (Laura, very good) and Allianz (good overall).The dealer (good) could not find the issue, so escalated to Polestar (France or Europe?) Tech.Some s/w upgrades were performed and the car suddenly worked!I would like to know what the issue was (is?), and it seems no one can tell me, and I'm unsure any updates are centrally reported.  This really doesn't inspire confidence in the car or the brand - it's SO frustrating, and many people worked really hard to resolve, which is why I've taken the time to write it up.Thanks again for all the support - fingers crossed

I’ve owned my P2 for nearly 4 years from new. When it’s working well, the car is just superb.  However, I’ve suffered multiple flat 12v batteries, the software seems to be getting slower and slower, and the AC onboard charger failed. It has taken two months to get the issue resolved with Polestar accepting 50% of the repair costs. On one extremely positive note, the customer service rep I’ve been dealing with on the OBC issue has been absolutely excellent. He has done everything in his power to get this resolved expeditiously and has been unfailingly courteous.

Car is very good although the side mirrors drive me mad. Also don't know why, but it appears that the service program (or schedule) is top secret - despite repeated requests they just won't provide it.Reply from Polestar below also not helpful - all I want is a sheet of paper with checklist of the work that needs doing at 18,000 miles, 36,000, 54,000 etc. But customer service says I just need to ask the garage, so anyway I have learnt the service intervals are 18,000 miles or 2 years (whichever is soonest) but it used to be 20,000 miles.

I am very happy with the car it is amazing to drive and well built (Volvo subsidary) however that said the Support side is terrible.I bought it secondhand 6 onths old very low mileage but wanted to find out about roof bars and towbar.PS Support told me to talk to the service centres, nearest are Toronto & Oakville. After mutliple emails no-one has bothered to respond.I just hope when it comes to service they will actually make the effort to talk to me.

Don’t really know where to start, car was due to be delivered on the 16th of March pm slot but got a call at 3 o’clock in the afternoon on the 15th of March (the day i paid to have my current car fully valeted and collected by the lease company) saying delivery would not be taking place due to the car not accepting a software update,was advised it would probably add a week or so to my delivery. Phoebe in their cs has been great and tried to get answers but I still have no idea when the car is coming or even if the software update has worked yet and tomorrow marks a month since the car should of arrived and I am still sat here with no car or clue when it might arrive. Update car now received and great have decided to up my star rating as although very frustrating having the delay, I do believe polestar were trying everything and especially Phoebe in cs who has moved heaven and earth over the last couple of weeks helping me
Very funny advert - inadvertently. Did they not think that using "I just can't get enough" as the slogan in their ad means most people will think *I just can't get enough charge"? !!!!

I have a 2023 Polestar 2 and it is a superb car. I've covered almost 27000 miles since I purchased it in October 2024 and have had no issues with it. The worst thing is having to deal with Volvo dealerships that have little interest/knowledge and are constantly trying to make money out of Polestar owners. On both occasions it was taken in for the service they attempted to charge despite it being included. The second service I raised an issue with vibration through the steering/floor, they suspect the issue is the foam breaking up in the tyre and insisted the tyre would need to be replaced at my expense. Polestar support have confirmed this is not the case and stated they will contact the dealer to confirm this. The dealer should replace the tyres and recover the cost from Michelin. Thank you to Sara from Polestar support who provided 5 star support. Polestar please educate the Volvo dealers we're forced to use so everyone gets the same level of service at every dealer.

I have just taken delivery of my new Polestar 2. I've been having an issue with the Mobile App so contacted their Customer Care Team through the online chat. I was being dealt with by Liam, who was very helpful to resolve my issue
Helpful person in CS team (Radia) trying to support me with my ongoing connectivity issues with my Polestar 4

I have a Polestar 3 Performance with Plus, Pilot, Nappa and HD lights. My delivery experience was very poor (arrived with lots of scratches and key fob not sync'd to the car). I did kick up a fuss and to be fair the dealer responsible for PDI did step up, collected my car, provided another PS 3 loan car and returned it within the expected time. I was also given a Electroverse charge credit. The Polestar customer service rep kept in touch throughout.Since then it "mostly" works really well. My issues have been fairly minor, the connection with CarPlay doesn't automatically connect, sometimes it has no mobile data connection (it usually comes back after a few minutes) and a couple of times it has lost its GPS location. This is despite the latest software update, which is meant to address these. Hopefully the next will continue to improve stability. I had similar issues with my previous Telsa a few years ago, when their platform was "less stable" than it is now.But I've had no issues with charging or locking/unlocking the car (whether by key fob or digital key on my phone).It is supremely comfortable to drive, is very quiet, handles very well indeed on a windy road and the build quality and materials are first rate, it's a very special place to sit. Much, much more premium than our last Model Y Performance and not a lot slower.Economy is "ok" for something so large/heavy. I can get over 3 miles/kwh on long motorway trips, doing 60-65mph. So easily more than 300 miles, if a bit less than the 350 mile claim. Mine has 22" wheels, the 20" wheel version can do 50 miles more (and has a slightly better ride).The B&W audio is amazing (part of the Plus pack), best I've ever had in a car by a long way, especially on well recorded/mixed Dolby Atmos content (try REM's Automatic for the People or Dire Straits, Brothers in Arms).Some people seem to have had major issues but I've done nearly 4000 miles without anything that would want me to give it back.

I was lucky enough to be dealt with by Josh at Polestar customer services. It’s solely due to his individual ethos for customer care that he help me with a long running care issue that he saw out to conclusion. Polestar need more colleagues like Josh who take customer care seriously and appreciate there is a high level of expectation on all aspects of the Polestar experience, given the premium brand segment it’s trying to break into.In more general terms, the experience of owning the Polestar 3 is best described as mixed. The car when it works is a dream and more than competes with comparable models in this segment. However it’s completely let down by the software. It’s only a year on that the software is largely now stable though basic advertised features of a £80k+ car don’t work.The other thing that lets Polestar down is the service model. They rely wholly on Volvo and as of now, Volvo aren’t really up to speed to deal with the volume and complexity of warranty issues.Polestar has so much potential, but if it’s to be a premium brand that competes it needs to compete across all areas, having a well engineered amazing car is not enough.

It’s been five months since I started driving my Polestar 4, coming from Tesla Model Y-and I have to say, P4 is a phenomenal car. It’s easily ten times better than the Tesla in many ways. The software could use a bit refinement, but overall, it’s an exceptional vehicle. The range is great, it’s effortless to drive, and even after 400 miles in a day, it doesn’t leave you feeling tired. The build quality is impressive-true luxury with a high-end interior, excellent sound system, and sleek aerodynamics. I’ve driven 6000 miles in five months and absolutely love it. There have been few minor software glitches, but nothing that bothered me. A quick reset fixed everything, just like rebooting a phone or computer. On one occasion I reached out to customer service through the Polestar app, they resolved my issue within ten minutes- it’s been flawless ever since. Thanks
Quickly resolved my ownership issue on the app, after a 90 min delay due to 'a disturbance'. Milly was polite and helpful

I love my Polestar 2 Car, it’s beautifully put together, blisteringly quick and a pleasure to drive.It’s not without glitches though - phantom ‘propulsion service required’ messages (meaning a wasted day off and trip to a garage to be told there’s nothing wrong), DAB refusing to connect, infotainment and maps initialising for way too long (and surely if you set the car to warm-up then the infotainment system should be ready when you get in?) - but overall my feelings for the car and the company are positive.The App, however, is pretty awful and unreliable, and really lets the car’s functionality down. Charging timing and pre-conditioning failures can give sleepless nights when an early start with a full battery is needed. I’ve been stood on the drive or sat in the car at 3am on numerous occasions trying to sort out why the car isn’t charging or online to communicate with charger.UPDATE: I have upped my rating from 3 to 4 and removed the comment about my biggest gripe (the ability to actually contact anyone), as their phone systems have been fine since my complaint and I later found that Polestar had replied, but their emails had gone into my spam folder, so they can't be blamed for that!
Very responsive, sound information and fixed both my issues promptly.

We purchased a polestar 4 and whilst we managed to sort everything out, we couldn’t access google assist. So we contacted polestar and assigned Laura, who guided us through what we needed to do, so now all working, thanks Laura
Got an instant response on webchat and the person I spoke with was really friendly and helpful!
Although I didn't really get all the info I was after, the response time was very good and the communication from the Southampton space has always been excellent.
Very helpful and efficient
Contacted Polestar today. Jacob B.Excellent support, although I am still waiting for a response, the service was excellent.
Kaori listened carefully to my issues with my Polestar 2 and ensured that my car was booked into the service centre.Follow up communication asking how things were progresing was useful.
Jacob from the customer service team has been great, very attentive and tried to help me resolve my issue

I love my new Polestar - to drive, to sit in.  But the software is infuriating.  However, the team are very helpful trying to resolve the issues.  Not their fault, but my new car (Polestar 3) just isn't ready for the the public quite yet!!!

I give Josh the polestar assistant 5* as he was friendly and very efficient. But I give my polestar3 only 4*. It’s a great car to drive, but small niggling problems with the informant screen and app are always present. You just get everything working as it should then another problem occurs. Nothing to stop you driving the car but very annoying as a lot of the time it’s offline and you don’t know how much charge is in the car.

I had a problem with connecting/accessing car climate control, location etc on my phone following it's first service. After many calls to Polestar help line, I was finally assigned to Laura.I can not praise her more highly, at no point did she try to dismiss by fobbing me off with a fake excuse. Laura made sure that my problem with my Polestar 2 was passed onto the technical team and she chased up with them until it was finally resolved.

Based on my Test Drive experience at Farnborough Exhibition Centre, I am very impressed by the PoleStar 2. Very friendly and efficient team who were most welcoming. Car is very good, although Google Assistant lost connection in an area not far from Farnborough (Tweseldown Race Course), but regained it once back on the main road. I'd consider, especially if they can produce a smaller car that would better suit my wife. Still have concerns re Range Anxiety, even with 240 miles of range, given our regular drives to Devon and less regular but periodic drives to Scotland and France.I do agree that colours on web-site and on the sample colour panels don't look much like the ACTUAL colours on the car. But I enjoyed the experience and it has given me something to think about.

Had a polestar 2 in 2022 as a company car , liked it a lot and missed it since it went back in 2024 hence just got my self an updated version SMLR last month and it’s even better . Had an issue with the app and pairing , called polestar support and found them very helpful , issue sorted in no time and even got a follow up call 📞! Thanks Jon.Amazing service + Awesome product
Great service, helped solve my problem in less than 5 minutes

I love my Polestar and was looking for my nearest service centre. The website searches, wouldn't load for some reason, however i contacted the help via the chat and they identified the nearest centres for me. Very quick, very responsive, I am a very happy customer.

Amazing support. I got straight through to Hamzah, who resolved my account  problem within a few minutes.That’s so refreshing these days. Apart from the reassurance that help is readily to hand, it also means I have the time to leave this review, as I didn't have to spend hours listening to hold music or trying to help a chatbot understand my problem and have it direct me to a page that I've read a hundred times before.

PS4 delivered today, so far a great experience.Really good comms via email, was apprehensive after reading some reviews however so long as you read the info and follow the instructions pre delivery the car then all worked on handover.Digital key set up with the handover guy and it’s all working nicely.Had to change initial drop off dates and this was done within 2 hours.Such a step up in quality from another famous EV brand!
I needed some help with setting up my new ownership, my problem was sorted out within a few minutes. Very helpful and prompt communication via on line chat.

Being a new owner of a Polestar 4 I contacted Polestar customer service with several questions. The experience was wonderful like a breath of fresh air, they went the extra mile giving me written solutions and helping me through setting up the car as a new owner, loving it. Keep the good work up Polestar [Radia]
Jon from support team did a great job supporting me with my questions regarding the car. I appreciate it!
Great support today on polestar chat. Had to process change of ownership. Handled smoothly & professionally by Erin.

I decided to lease the VinFast VF8 primarily due to the promotional incentives being offered at the time. I also had negative equity in my previous vehicle, and the dealership was able to work with me to make the transition possible. I was excited—it was my first EV, and I really wanted to support a newer automaker. Unfortunately, the experience has been frustrating from the start.Electrical Issues:I encounter electrical problems almost daily. The blind spot detection sensors frequently stop working, despite the car being garage-kept and cleaned regularly. The indicator stays illuminated in both mirrors with no real reason why.The SOS system is another ongoing issue—it sends repeated alerts that the service is inactive, then clears itself, only to return a few minutes later. On my 20-mile commute, I’ll hear the notification sound at least 10 times each way. The car also makes random warning chimes throughout the drive, often too briefly for me to catch what’s wrong before the alert disappears. These could be related to the collision sensors or other systems—it’s hard to tell.The driver-side master window controls also don’t work properly. When I attempt to roll up the windows, most of them don’t respond on the first try. I often need to press the switch three or four times. Using the auto-up feature results in the car acting like something is blocking the window—even when nothing is there.The comfort access feature (keyless entry via door handle) is extremely inconsistent, working maybe 30% of the time. When it doesn’t work, it defeats the entire purpose of having it.Charging is another pain point. Instead of simply unlocking the car to stop the charge, I have to enter the vehicle, manually end the charging session, then get out and unplug it. This should be a much smoother process.VinFast partners with Autel for their wall-mounted chargers, but getting support from them has been frustrating. They take no ownership of the issues I’ve had and often push the problem back to the dealership. It also feels like you’re pressured into buying the Level 2 charger since charging the vehicle from 60% to 80% using a standard outlet can take nearly 24 hours.Even small things like startup behavior become annoying: the second you tap the brake to power on the car, you’re bombarded with seatbelt alerts. There’s no grace period like in most vehicles.Apple CarPlay is also unreliable, only working about 70% of the time.Interior Issues:The rearview mirror warps and distorts everything behind you. It’s hard to describe, but it makes vehicles look oddly stretched or distorted—almost like funhouse mirrors. I’m not sure if it’s the glass quality or a design flaw.Mechanical Issues:The ride quality is very poor. The suspension feels dated—like something from an early 2000s car. It’s stiff, loud, and does a poor job of absorbing road imperfections. It also doesn’t handle corners well at all.Folding down the rear seats is simple enough, but putting them back up requires a surprising amount of strength. I’m able to manage, but I can see this being a real challenge for older drivers or anyone with limited mobility.Accessing the front trunk (frunk) is also unnecessarily difficult. You need to pull the lever five times with considerable force just to get it to release—it honestly feels like something might break.Final Thoughts:There may be more issues I’m forgetting, but overall, I’d rate this car a 2 out of 10. Communication with my local VinFast dealership has been difficult due to constant staff turnover, and dealing with Autel for charger issues has been equally frustrating.For a company that had access to the EV landscape and a chance to learn from more established brands, VinFast has released something that feels rushed and unfinished. I understand they’re a young company, but after nearly a decade in development, this vehicle should be much more refined—especially at a price point near $50,000. I wouldn’t choose this car again, and I can’t recommend it in its current state.

On Sunday, January 18th, I charged just over 15 kWh at an Equans charging station in Amsterdam using the Electroverse app. The price per kWh was €0.35. Total cost was €5.29.When the charging session started, €10 was debited from my bank account. After the session ended, €4.71 was refunded, so the total amount I paid was €10 - €4.71 = €5.29. Both amounts were booked on January 19th, 2026, with the transaction date of January 18th and the value date of January 19th.The next day, another €5.29 was debited from my account. This time with the booking date of January 20th, but with the transaction date of January 20th and the value date of January 18th.On January 26th, the €4.71 refunded on January 19th was credited back (correction) to Electroverse: the transaction date was January 26th and the value date was January 19th.So, the net amount debited from my account wasn't €5.29, but €15.29, €10 too much. A debit of €10, a credit of €4.71, a debit of €5.29, and a debit of €4.71.Mistake or fraud?Of course, I assumed it was a mistake, but the behavior of Electroverse's service staff has led me to conclude otherwise. I informed Electroverse of what happened, as described above. A clear explanation. I sent Electroverse copies of the relevant bank statements. I sent Electroverse copies of teh bank statements with all the amounts Electroverse debited from my account in January 2026. In January 2026, I loaded a total of six times with the Electroverse app, for a total amount of €46.84. The bank statements I sent Electroverse clearly show that a total of €56.84 was debited from my account in January: €10 too much.Not a difficult case, right?I first contacted Electroverse about this on January 28, 2026. Since then, I've contacted Electroverse 10 times (!) and sent copies of bank statements and downloads of my bank account several times.I've asked them to check their own records to see which amounts were debited from my account and compare them with the charging sessions, but to date, they have categorically refused. At their request, I've contacted my bank twice, but the bank says that they can't reverse the charges and that only Electroverse can, since they initiated the debits. Although they've already received all the relevant bank statements, they keep asking for new ones. Also considering a negative review on January 23, 2026 from David on Trustpilot who had a similar experience, I've concluded that this could be a case of fraud.Ad David pointed out to other users of Electroverse in his review: check your statements.I will inform the board of directors as well as the external auditor about this potential case of fraud.Case resolved. No fraud, thankfully.

I was only able to prevent unauthorized charges because I used PayPal, which allowed me to block the automatic payments from my own banking interface.• Incompetent Support: It has been 4 months since I opened a ticket. Stay away if you still can!Anything you can think of went wrong with the installation - from the scaffolders putting bars in front of our neighbours door (without permission) to the agreed place inside the house being too small for the battery to our roof being too small for the panels. They then put some on our neighbours roof (until my husband pointed that out). I could go on and on, eg one day they turned up with a ladder too short for the scaffolding  Our walls were damaged in several places through the scaffolding - took them weeks to remove the scaffolding and make good of the damage. But the worst: the panels worked for about a month and stopped about 4 months ago. Since, we've been chasing and chasing and no one gets back to you! We've finally had an engineer out a month ago, told us the inverter was faulty and we'd get a new one installed. Then 10 days ago another engineer saying that the panels might be faulty, knowing nothing of the feedback of engineer 1. And again - no word from Egg since!

UPDATE October 2025================Egg agreed they got it wrong. They have agreed to pay for my chosen Tesla contractor to rectify the faults in the installation.  I just need to send them the invoice.They have withdrawn from solar PV market at this time, but will run a small support group for existing installations.My new contractor is surveying and measuring for cabling this week, then will erect scaffold and start their rectification work by removing all panels and re-wiring correctly to the inverter; and then installing the right protection into the Gateway.
Very poor service would not recommend

Awful company, they initially installed the charger and it worked fine until recently it stopped working.I have been contacting Egg for weeks now but to no avail. Two zoom meetings, wasted time struggling with a faulty log in and when I politely requested help I  was told they were not taking any new orders, which is odd, because they are.What a load of ....

Engineers are great but customer service is absolutely shocking. Took so many phone calls to eventually get stuff sorted. This is due to a faulty charger (which was installed for a few months). Excellent sales executive (Freddie Marlow), checking what we needed and if it would be the most cost effective. 5 star rating.Excellent solar insulation. All panels fitted over half a day. 5 star ratingElectrical installation went well once they had all the parts (they didnt know they were installing an eddi), and replaced a faulty trip. Although, couldn't get the Eddi to register, can't fault the effort they put in. But product knowledge was required here. Took a visit by the technical side a few days later to eventually get it sorted. 3 star ratingHowever, awful after care/delivery team communication as per many of the 1 star ratings.Sent numerous emails to the after care guys. Nothing. Took one email to the sales executive, who responded instantly and got things organised so questions were answered and all the necessary documents sent. 1 star rating.So Egg, You are majorly let down by your after care/delivery team. If this was a school ofsted inspection you would have been rated inadequate!!

I purchased some Egg Solar thinking I’d get some sunny-side-up energy savings, but boy was I in for a scrambled mess!Their sales rep promised my panels would be “egg-cellent” and kept saying their installation would be “over easy.” Well, it was over alright - over budget, over schedule, and over my head in frustration!The installation crew showed up three hours late because apparently they had to “crack dawn” first. When they finally arrived, half the panels were cracked (not the good kind of crack). I asked if they were fresh, and the foreman just shrugged and said “they’re not exactly farm-to-roof.”Then came the real disaster. These geniuses managed to drill clean THROUGH my roof into my living room! I now have a lovely skylight above my couch that I definitely didn’t order. When I pointed this out, the crew leader said “well, at least you’ll get more natural light!” Thanks, but I was hoping the light would come from the PANELS, not the gaping hole in my ceiling.But wait, it gets better! As they were leaving, their van backed straight into my BMW. The driver hopped out, looked at my dented bumper, and had the audacity to say “looks like your car got egg-ed!” I was not amused. Apparently their reversing skills are about as good as their drilling precision.Two months later, my energy bill is still through the roof - which is ironic since that’s literally where they put the holes! When I called customer service about the roof damage AND my car, they kept putting me on hold saying they were “still cooking up a solution.”The final straw was when they told me the warranty doesn’t cover “acts of chickens” OR “vehicular mishaps during yolk-ing around.” WHAT?!Save yourself the headache and shell out your money elsewhere. These guys are just bad eggs who’ll crack your roof AND your ride.**Would not recommend unless you enjoy playing whack-a-mole with water leaks and dealing with insurance claims.**-----

Unfortunately I have had issues since since day 1.  Scaffolding arrived without notice, double the amount of panels delivered, sub contracted team turned up then left after half an hour as they didn’t have the right equipment, came back another day and fitted whole kit in 3 hours, my brother had them their for 4 days!! obviously, it wasn’t correct, no usage directions given by the team just a handbook.  The system wasn’t set up correct and we were paying for the battery to to filled up so our meter was doing somersaults, after a number of emails we finally got a technician visit who corrected their mistake, we had to chase up the handover back, eventually got it minus the DNO which was apparently on its way, the only thing they seem bothered about is getting their money, we are 6 months down the line and still do not have the DNO so can’t sell extra electric back to our supplier we have generated over £300 that we will be out of pocket, I’m sick of sending emails without success, we don’t even get a reply now, absolutely shocking service I WOULD NOT RECOMMEND EGG them and if EGG read this please get in touch, for the last 4 weeks I’ve been ignored.

I entered a 3-year finance agreement with Egg for an EV charger sold as a package, including their branded Egg Plus support. Almost a year early, they unilaterally withdrew this service, leaving me paying for a product that no longer matches what I was sold. Their excuses were contradictory and dishonest — claiming the product was “retired” whilst still selling it on their website, and pretending that an unproven manufacturer warranty somehow replaced their own contract. They then ignored my formal complaint completely for over 8 weeks. A textbook case of mis-selling, material breach of contract, and appalling customer service. Now with the Financial Ombudsman.

The sales and installation process went smoothly and was carried out to a high standard – no complaints there. The team was professional throughout. However, after the installation was completed, it became increasingly difficult to get in touch with the project coordinator to set up our battery charging schedules to take advantage of off-peak grid hours. Despite multiple attempts via phone and email, I’ve yet to receive a response. Additionally, the setup guide provided was for a different app and not relevant to SolaXCloud, which added to the confusion. Overall, the experience with the company was positive, but this feedback specifically relates to the project coordinator and the support/training provided after installation.

I'm leaving this review to see if I can get a response about my MCS certificate which I believe has a mistake regarding 'DNC' figure and is therefore unusable for a feed in tarifAfter 2-3 weeks of numerous emails and phone calls to egg with no response so farI'm hoping they may respond on hereIf that is the case I may ammend my review -

My experience with egg started off positively — the sales process was efficient, and everything was clearly explained. I have a car charger from them 2 years now or something. The charger itself works but the internet connection is terrible. When it comes to selling they are very organised and call you numerous amount of time because they need business from you.
