On 31st January when installer came to install the panel I asked him and he has spoken to his boss and I received a call from Mac from solar and he explained so I said why no one has came back until now because if this is the case then I wouldn’t go ahead with the project so could you please ask Harry sales advisor to give me a call back. Harry did rang me same morning and offered £500 refund for 2 panel which has been disagreed at the end as he said that’s maximum they normally offer because they have to cover the cost of scaffolding and etc. I asked him weather you sell 12 panels or 14 panels scaffolding price is always same so if you have not managed to fit 2 panels on my roof why should I been charged for the scaffolding so he said he will go and speak to his manager and comeback to me by this evening.It’s been 15 days since that conversation and numerous phone call and emails been sent but as usually no response. There is issue with scaffolding which has been already reported by email that also has been ignored.Finally Scaffolding came down today (16/02/25) after left the review but still no response regarding 2 panels.I think I need to take advice from ofgam regarding this issue as lack of communication from Egg.

It took two attempts to get a dual pole isolator installed and then a further two attempts to get the charger installed.Everyone was friendly and helpful on the phone. Professional and customer-focussed. I think that they are working with a number of small contractors who don't perhaps have the wide reach yet that is needed.The charger works, although I'm not sure how to set it up the way I would like it to.

The deal and installation and panels etc are ok but the support afterwards is horrific. Our installation was completed end of November and the paperwork is still not done! And I see no response in regards to the paperwork that’s prohibiting us from getting an export tariff!

All that 'interest free' advertising is priced into their quotes. Had a very disappointing experience with their sales team, who were eager to slag off other installers (proof), despite the competitors actually having better trust pilot ratings.Stick to an honest company, like Octopus.
Garbage 'fast' charge.In Tynemouth and had to sit in car waiting. What should have taken 50 minutes took 2 hours and battery was still only on 50%. Cost 40 quid. Rip off and will never use Egg again.

The process from order (placed on the 23/07) to installation was protracted, poorly managed and communicated: Order was placed on the 23/07. The installation itself went very well on the 18/10. I have little confidence in their internal feedback system as Egg has yet to contact me on this regard.

Still waiting for someone to contact me to get the isolator switch fitted or at least arranged. Initial experience and setup was good but recent experiences have not been great. Feels a little bit like once they have your money they’re reluctant to give you any attention and focus instead on winning new customers.
A lot of emails back and fourth we wanted to have two installs one at my property and one at my parents, parents gave up I continued but took for ever to get installed

Checked online and the deal for a charger on a 3 year payment plan on 0% APR looked amazing so registered my interest. They have contacted me and asked for a survey which I did. I asked if they could install a isolator switch (which they advised they could through their partners) and they told me someone would be in touch to arrange this.2 weeks after with no Comms I decided to reach out as I haven't heard anything and they advised there was some backlog but they were trying to bump me into priority.1 week after I reach out, to be told "they have tried to call you a few days ago but no answer". Checked my phone and no missed calls or voicemails. Asked if they had my correct number (which they did) and if I could get their contact number and call them instead to get it booked. No answer.Fast forward one more week and I ask again what's the status on this, this time they tell me they have a massive backlog so they didn't manage to get in touch since my initial enquiry (so they didn't call me after all?)Ended up going with another provider which I applied on a Friday, got a quote on Monday, paid on Tuesday and on Wednesday they booked me for the isolator fitting and the charger the week after, which has now been installed. Cancelled everything with Egg, which is a shame because they did sound really promising
My Indra charger does not connect to the App but it is up to me to contact Indra - did I not purchase the unit from Egg ? -  why aren’t you dealing with this issue ?

Trying to do all of the reams of checks needed and the lack of proactivity from the Egg contact was not good, and i really got frustrated from the lack of understanding of me and my needs. However, the unit is brilliant and the installation was great - professionally installed, etc
The whole process needs updatingI was told the initial survey would  take 5mins and 3 photos.  I live in an old property and no one informed me of the problems I would face.

The process of getting the charger fitted was excellent. Also your website has a fantastic customer journey. Super easy to use and clear.However there has been lack of support since. I have 3 outstanding enquiries. I feel that the EggPlus service is a bit of a con, chargers come with warranty. Also the charging platform has not been white labelled, which seems strange.
Off line purple light flashing I understand your working to resolve

You've asked so here goes.Placing order - very easy. Few photos and a couple of emails and was all booked in.Installation- was booked for the 21st of February. Got a call a week before saying could they come and do a first fix. We eventually agreed on the Saturday before. No where did it say and I wasn't told it would take 2 visits to get the charger installed. Which I think is poor communication as arrangements were made to have the day off on the 21st for it to be installed luckily one day was a weekend don't think it's right you have to take 2 days off work for a 3hour install. Then received a call on the 18th of Feb asking to come on the 19th as the engineer had requested a day off. The install went ok. Seemed very rushed. On the second visit the main connection was done and commissioned. The charger connected to the app but no real help or guidance on how it all worked. If your not tech savvy I could imagine it being a bit overwhelming. For the cost it's ok but since then I believe there has been a price increase and don't think it's worth the new additional costs.Overall it's ok. Time will tell what the after care is like.
Ok,App is not the best I have seen.I only used EGG through the recommendation of a friend.I maybe wished I had shopped around.

*update* Chris the Egg engineer has attended and sorted out the issues. A full reinstall was required with all new kit and cable. It's amazing how the old lot worked for so long. Anyway. Happy now, Chris has restored my faith in Egg slightly,he worked tirelessly for near 5 hours without a break, he carried on until he was happy he had done the best he could 5* for Chris 3* for Egg, only due to the fact the initial install was shoddy.What a crock of S**t. Indra ev charger has not been right since it was installed. I gave up getting engineers out and just worked it off the boost button. Not even a year old and it's packed up working altogether. Indra and Egg support is closed for the weekend, great customer service that is. I now have to wait till Monday to speak to someone,  then who knows how long for an engineer to come out. In the meantime I am without a car.

The price for the charger (Egg badged Indra Smart Pro) and install is good. Was originally higher when I signed up but price came down and, when asked, Egg reissued the contract so I would pay the new, lower price.Install was ok…. It’s a photo survey and they didn’t pick up that I needed an isolator installed by the network operator. Day of install was ok but had to call out electrician as something was out of place in fuse board which was noticed after engineer left - Egg refunded me the cost of the electrician‘s call out and fix charge. The instruction from the engineer was to scan a QR code and download an app - turns out the app wasn’t ‘live’ so only a web interface available for the first few months. Overall not a brilliant experience with the install.Charger worked fine for a couple of months then packed in. Took a good few weeks to be sorted with an engineer visit and the solution was to replace the charger.Since then it has mostly been doing as expected but in the last few months has intermittent connectivity issues which need one or more reboots to fix. The app is ok and easy enough to use. Customer support seems better now and emails will be answered over the weekends.Overall the price is quite good but I guess you get what you pay for and I can’t say the charger is as reliable as I would have hoped for. Not having confidence that your charger’s worked overnight and you may not have the expected battery level isn’t great.
Burst brick no drip loop on external cable allowing water to runto house no sealent on external hole drilled through my gate post not at side as requested

The installation team were great. However we were told the team would arrive at 9am, they didn’t come until 11.40 which completely ruined our plans for the afternoon. The customer service team member was a bit passive aggressive in her email to us. But, the actual installation team were really good and our appliance (so far) is working on well.
Installation went very smoothly. However, the Indra app is very slow almost useless.

I was disappointed that it took so long to set up charger appointment due to the fact Egg does not have site appraisals. I live in the South East which I suspect is a problem for Egg?

Slow customer service all through email.Had a Indra charger installed waited for 3-4 weeks for installation,once installed all fine BUT wasn’t linked with the app waited days for app integration once sorted all good for a few days then stopped working could only use boost button only firstly in charger and  no app integration once again so couldn’t take advantage of cheap off peak rates overnight.Had to wait another 4 weeks for a install guy to fix problem turned out WIFI connection kept failing even through I had strong WiFi outside,so they swapped over a WiFi connection for a mobile signal connection which lasted 24 hours then went offline again now it’s back been great for a few weeks now.The concept is good the app and linking to ev charger is poor and needs a good software update

I really wanted to feel grateful and positive the whole way through, but I have to be honest and say that we've been rather disappointed. All went very well untill after the installation of the indra car charger. It worked brilliantly for a few days. Then when I had to start using a new phone, I couldn't transfer the Indra app onto it. Instead I found I had connected with a new version,  that obviously wasn't aware of Pedro's introduction of charger to car. Pedro was the installer and he had had to do something clever to make charger and car speak nicely to one another.Then started the problem of who to contact.  Egg? Indra? Or McNalleys the installers? It was very difficult to get either of the first two on the telephone. Gareth from Egg kindly helped me connect with Indra.  Then two helpful people from Indra tried to repair the problem remotely without success. Finally McNalleys rang to say they were coming ( more than a week into the future). Meanwhile we're scared to try using the charger in case it damages/confuses the car's computer, since the charger is very confused itself. It won't do solar charging . Once itdid it when I'd set it to boost!!I just think there are too many directons to look in for help  and that it would be good if Egg acted as traffic officer for the public, especially as we are paying the extra per month from them for an efficient and speedy response system for any problems!

Egg are proving to be the company with zero customer service.  I have had nothing but problems trying to speak to anyone or answer emails and that is before any install, which is less and less likely.They have tried to charge me more for the install stating that the cable in my meter cupboard needs amending despite being a new build and seeing my survey photos when I originally booked the install in early May.  That was cancelled due to lack of contact but I thought I would give them another chance.  Unfortunately I have wasted my time.How can I trust a company to help me in the future if they can’t even answer simple questions now.Over all an extremely disappointing experience all round.06/06/23Following on from my previous review I wanted to update this and say that my faith in this company has been somewhat restored mainly due to the work of Ilona Rothwell.  Ilona has single-handedly taken ownership of the situation and followed up on the install to make sure all went well.  She is a credit to Egg.  Let’s hope all runs smoothly from here on.

Starting from answering queries about EV charging point features to installation to resolve issue has been less than satisfactory. Egg didn't have a way to book installation appointment. Third party used for installation didn't turn up on the day they said they would install. On reporting that Egg didn't had any information on the installation. Contractor took two days for the standard installation while website didn't mention this not did the contractor before the day of installation. After installation contractor didn't show how it can be used despite asking. Issue of using app took ages to resolve. Overall not too pleased with the experience so far.

*Update Egg took control of the situation and expedited a service engineer, which I am extremely grateful for.The individual staff from Egg, Indra and the service team went above and beyond when they realised the issues I was facing and I am very grateful for each of them.Issue now resolved with great care from Egg team - Thank you.Issue with Charger since 24 MAR since software update. New charger replaced today, but do not have data feed.I have been told continuously by Egg, who I pay that I have functionality, although I have went through troubleshooting steps with Indra which shows I have not.After replacement today from the third third party involved, I am told by Egg I am back online, which I have no data feed or resolution from Indra software team yet.Egg charge you, hold your contract but hire a third party for install and another third party for troubleshooting. Then if there are any issues hold little accountability to them and pass the buck.Poor show Egg on this one, I am currently £223 down  having no home charging facility for nearly 4 weeks, and now I have charging but no data feed, which will continually cost me until resolution due to my work paying directly via Mina with data feed. Case number 71819.
I was happy in the beginning with the way the purchase was handled but since its been installed I have been waiting nearly 2 weeks for someone to come and sort the connection so the app works

The unit when working is fine. The programming has been an issue since installation and waiting to be fixed.
Charging unit fried it's self, unit was replaced but this a continuation of problems I've encountered since I signed up in December 2022
Not used the new app as I have the OVO EV charge app and you do not recommend changing.

Edit - after some weeks of dodgy customer support from Indra, the situation is resolved. But it does not leave me with high hopes for a good customer support experience from both companies. Slow communication.I was expecting replies to emails and phone calls sooner than they have been.Due to egg delaying the email communication, I was told that I've missed the installation date that I wanted, which was no fault of mine. Admittedly the electrics at my house needed upgrading, which were done fairly pronto.

Experience was great overall. I went with EGG over my energy supplier Octopus as EGG were far more competitive on price and the Octopus sales approach so bad at one point I thought it a scam! (I will add that a different story for Air Heat Pump where Octopus performed well and they installing mine now). With EGG  the initial calls and the talk through of options was clear, informstive and friendly. They do lose one star as unfortunately, when I was passed through to the call rep responsible for the installation part they were dreadful. No apology given but they blase rep and the scaffolding firm said this happens often! System up and running and seems working well 2 months in.

Happy with egg and the speed of installation, it took a bit longer due to wanting more than 10 panels so egg needed to apply to the grid for the correct paperwork so they couldn’t start until they had this in place. As soon as the paperwork g99 was approved we were offered a couple of dates to start and the scaffolding went up the week before.  A few teething problems with the scaffolding as we needed on all three sides of the house but was sorted fairly quickly . Electricians did a neat job of the install and after a few days we had solar and started reducing our electricity bill, the app is easy to use and you can see the solar energy we are now producing. Overall egg were quick to solve issues were polite and friendly in communication and have delivered what we expected.

Service through quote and install was great. Very knowledgeable team members who know their product well. Service did slip after install, and communication could be improved here but it wouldn’t stop me using egg again, just something to be aware of. I had to chase details a few times.

A pretty trouble free process. The pre-purchase and pre-installtion conversations went well. There was an issue with our order which was sorted about ten days after install. Overall the panels etc have reduced our electricity use and spend by around 90%(from a weekly average of 81 kwh use from the grid to about 0.5 at the mo) the remaining 10% is the standing charge.

Really pleased with my set up, panels and battery, Mia and the team have been outstanding… but of advice go big on panels, they cost too much to retro fit and be all over export, DNO takes ages to get sorted out… SP are the ones who slow it, egg have been amazing help
Slight delay with install mainly due to power company but liaison could have been slightly better. Good product, great service and the 3rd party installer was eggcellent.

Very good service now that the charger is fitted. One of the main disappointments are when you called there is no answer and you have to email. Sometimes they can take 2 to 3 days to respond. I write this mixed emotions as I want to give Egg 5 stars but I can’t.I organised for a battery to be installed at my mother’s house.  She already had solar so a battery was the logical next step.  There was bit of chasing up I had to do to get the installation date organised but no major dramas.  Installation day went well, it was really good, the installers did a great neat, quick, job. The app was installed on my mum’s phone but how it worked wasn’t really explained to her. Not a problem as I could sort this out and get the app installed on my phone too. So far so good, the I started looking at the SOC of the battery which only ever charged from her PV, it never seemed to get over 20%. Not a problem, I could sort that- I moved my mum’s tariff onto Octopus Flux in preparation for cheaper overnight electricity. I then tried to program the app to mirror the times of the tariff. I couldn’t do that as 3 firmware upgrades were needed first, why weren’t they done at installation?  Email to Egg to get that sorted. Firmware upgrades complete and now I could program the app but still no overnight charge. Hmmm. Another email to Egg, someone from Egg was looking into it and the crux is after 2 weeks of tooing and froing, still no overnight charge and someone speaking to Solax to find out the problem why.  I’m know some of this isnt Egg’s fault, but I’ve had to do a lot of chasing up and the problem still isn’t resolved. The installation was faultless but the after-sales leaves a lot to be desired.  I’m sure I will get there in the end but to the average person who wants to do the right thing and take advantage of cheap, clean, renewable energy, this isn't what I would call a smooth operation.  I have a good understanding of this technology, a lot of people don’t have and need for this to be done as efficiently and smoothly as possible or it won’t work and the wonder of solar and batteries will be a negative experience and will put people off when we need everyone to transition as quickly as possible.  My request would be for better after sales and stress free commissioning of systems.
They got on with the job ,perfectly.

The installation team were great and this went without hassle. Excellent system and competitive price. The communication around the start of the project and speed of receiving documentation following install needs some work.
Kiera Taylor very clear explanation
The only problem I had was the wait for my charger to be put in but understand why. Once in I have had no problems so far with my charger.
Need to make simple  prices one engineered should complete process not in three different days not three different people

Update 25/11/2024So since my original review I am happy to report that Egg stepped up and resolved all my issues, including completing the groundworks and repairing the minor damage to the brickwork caused by drilling the holes for the cables coming from the roof.Thanks to Mia and Charlene for sorting everything out, and thanks to all the subcontractors hired by Egg to do the works. I am very happy with the panels and batteries now up and running, and already enjoying the benefits of both, despite the dull weather!Update 11/11/2024Since writing my original (and lengthy) review last week I have had contact from Charlene who is the head of delivery at egg and they have given an excellent response to all my concerns. I still as yet do not have a date for the remainder of the work to be completed however I am feeling more confident that this will be addressed to my satisfaction. I will update the review to reflect the final outcome.****************So everything started off well; good appointment when inquiring, pretty good communication after agreeing and signing documents.Spoke to my project coordinator on Monday with the solar install agreed for that Thursday and Friday where we discussed the groundworks necessary as the batteries and inverter are being installed in the detached garage so we wanted a small amount of groundworks to bury the cables in the garden. Said this would be no problem and I followed this up with an email and detailed photos showing where I wanted the cable to run in the garden.Scaffolders came on Tuesday and it was a good job I was home as the date and time they were coming had not been communicated other than possibly 24/48 hours before install or possibly even on the day. Not a big issue though so up the scaffolding went.Day of the install the subcontractors hired by Egg came promptly and were great, however nothing at all had been arranged for the groundworks so the electrician called up head office and was advised to carry on as much as they could while Egg would look at a quote for the groundworks. Egg then emailed me (a different project coordinator this time as my original coordinator was on leave) and said they would look into getting a quote for the groundworks and a date.Panels were up quickly and I contacted Egg as I was obviously concerned that I had not been advised there was going to be an extra cost for the small amount of groundworks and this should, according to their contract, form part of the pre-install checks. Egg told me to leave it with them.At this point all the main install work has been done however I have a non-functioning system as no groundworks have been done to connect the system to the cables and the electrician cannot connect the panels up as he can’t leave live DC cables hanging off the side of the house with nowhere to go.I emailed Friday morning and then found my replacement coordinator was off today from the out of office I got, so I called the main number, explained the issue, and was told someone would call me back. No one of course has called me back and I’ve had no emails either so I have absolutely no idea what is happening.As far as I’m concerned Egg have not completed their pre-install checks, despite me communicating exactly what I wanted done and discussed groundworks with me prior to any work or scaffolding occurring, now I’m under the impression I’m going to be paying extra at a point in the process where I have absolutely no ability to refuse the work necessary and go with another provider.I find this really poor behaviour and would certainly recommend anyone considering using Egg to think hard about that.I will say the subcontractors they hired have been great, the work looks neat and tidy and they’ve done everything they can.Very frustrated overall though that during the install I’ve had very little contact and certainly none that wasn’t prompted by myself first, passed around between project coordinators, and I’m left with a system not connected as well as no answer to who is resolving the groundworks or when.

The ordering process was dealt with very impressively and courteously by IIona , with regular helpful guidance and assistance , thank you IIona. I would highly recommend Egg for any solar installation.

The timing of the service IMO is not scheduled correctly.I ordered the Cord charger on 20.01.2026.You get asked your preferred installation date which I chose 27.01.2026. It then goes on to tell you it's likely to be a 2-week wait. So why ask what date you want with the date options available to choose?Then you get asked to video where you think your cable should go. Now I'm out of the home from 07:00 to 19:00 and in January it's dark both before 07:00 and after 19:00, so it's impossible for me to video until the weekend.I then send the video in on Saturday 24th Jan and received the "it's approved" email on Monday 26th. This email goes on to basically say you're probably 7-10 days away from any kind of action as there are approval waiting times and delays for the DNO approval.I still have Jan 27th as my booked date but this comes & goes without any contact from Cord.You then start receiving garbled emails from the National Grid.Then you get calls and emails about whether the DNO has contacted you. By this time I've forgotten who or what a DNO is ("distribution network operator" apparently) and I'm trawling through previous emails looking to see if I do indeed have this.Then from the video you sent in and the questionnaire you have to complete, it turns out that the £850.00 I've already paid isn't sufficient to cover the cost of my installation and I have to pay another £414.00. This was on February 2nd.I finally got my installation on February 18th.I will say the installer was great. Lovely fella. But he's contracted by Cord. He's not from Cord themselves.Just begs the question why they ask for your preferred date only to finally install it 3 weeks later!Plus there's no gauge to increased cost and you're stuck once you're in the process as you need a charger for the electric vehicle you've just bought but can't move very far!!

Installer raised a support issue this morning and now await update from the CORD tech team. *Update:- I received a call from the MD apologising after this review, so hopefully they will improve going forward, he seemed genuine* Seems a good product now installed, however, the booking of installation was a nightmare, I went with Cord because I was told typically installed within 2 weeks and I was going with Octopus but they stated 3 weeks for a survey and a further 5 to fit and I wanted sooner if possible, all good at first, I ordered end of July and was given a calendar to pick a 5 day window, I choose the first available 5 days, first week of August and booked a week off work as I had jobs to do at home as well to tie in, after chasing for a date, I was told nothing available until 28th August which was annoying, what's the point of the calendar offering you dates, they said they dont use that, I guess to make you commit to buy! I got confirmation email and phone call confirming 28th between 8:30-9am although they did say they may turn up as late as 11am which was fine, however, 12 noon comes and I call them to be told no idea why they haven't turned up they will check. 30 minutes later I get a call saying they messed up scheduling and wont be arriving, great, a wasted day off work hanging around! I was offered first job of the day the following Monday, I double checked arrival time and assured first job of the day!  Monday arrives and I am up, ready for the early arrival then receive a text saying the engineer will contact me at 11am with an ETA! He texts he will be there 13:45-14:00, not ideal as I had booked my work calls for 3pm,4pm and 5pm which I had to postpone and one of them called back to say they had gone with another company, so to sum up, good neat job in the end but I lost time off work and money due to lack of turning up when they said they would!

Following order we were quickly contacted for the online survey and agreed 3 extra metres of cable and were quoted £5-10 per metre. Made payment and then told it would be £12 per metre and an extra 5 would be needed and a further hour labour. Luckily we hadn't paid for the extra hour as install only took 2 and the extra 5 metres was only 3 as originally agreed. However, the installation was Thursday 17th July and returned with our new car Friday evening to discover it would not charge. There is no help over a weekend so had to wait until Monday. The technical team said it had been incorrectly set up so explained how we could reset the unit. On trying to charge the car on my return Monday night it still wouldn't charge and so called again Tuesday to be told to raise a ticket and had to wait four hours for a call back to be told it looked like the CT clamp is incorrectly installed and only charging at 1.3KW and still wont connect to my new car. Waited for a call for when the engineer would visit which didn't happen. Called again today to be told they would visit in a weeks time on Wednesday 30th and fix 'under warranty'. This is simply not good enough, it has not worked since it was fitted and to be expected to wait two weeks from the date of installation to have someone to look at it,whilst I pay for expensive public charging is not the level of service I expected.Update - the engineer turned up a week later following our review and has now rectified the issue. All has worked well since.

Since installation I've been chasing both Cord and the installers for the certification paperwork without success with both companies saying the other will provide it. Its really not that hard a task but no-one seems to want to take ownership of the issue.Update: you will see by the reply below that CORD EV continue to not understand my problem. As I have explained to them several times, I have already received an email from their installer saying the Certificates are attached. But they were not attached. Simple solution is to resend the email and actually ATTACH the attachments ! Come on CORD, pleaseUpdate: 25/01/26Eventually received my Installation Cerificate.However, the Charger unit is now repeatedly going into an error status, reading the orange and red lights would indicate an insufficient or excess voltage or similar so will contact Cord on Monday to request an engineer. The latest fault occurred overnight when the car was plugged in. Woke to an insufficiently charged car ! Not greatUpdate: 27.01.2026having contacted CORD about the charger faulting several times, it seems the unit voltage protection was activating at between 243 and 246v, despite it apparently being able to accept 253v before going into disconnection. CORD advised my to go into the set-up pages and up the voltage limit to 262v. Great !  Unfortunately I am unable to get into the app set-up pages as the 4 digit pin on the unit is not correct ! That's going to be a problem if I ever need to change broadband provider and change to a new router. The customer service agent advised to press the physical pin reset button on the unit, however the Cord Lite (rebranded Cord One) doesn't have one. The instruction manual at 15.3 is incorrect and has been copied over from the Cord Zero unit. Lazy and plain wrong.So, if I don't start getting answers and better service (ie engineer attendance) I will be requesting a full refund. I have adjusted the review from 3 stars to 2 accordingly.Update 04.02.2026Today I was contacted by Cord to try a new PIN on the app to change setting on the charger. This WORKED ! I am now able to access the setting menu and, as instructed, changed the default unit protection voltage from 453v to 462.2v. I will monitor this to see if it makes any difference.I WOULD URGE ALL USERS TO CHECK THAT THEY CAN ACCESS THE BACK APP USING THE PIN SUPPLIED ON THEIR UNITS. Worse case scenario is that your charger may not work if you change broadband providerUpdate 10.02.26Since amending the voltage protection setting, the unit is working fine it would appear.It would have saved a lot of hassle, stress and time if Cord's installer (24/7EV) had told me they had changed the pin during installation and what they had changed it to.I will update from 2 to 3 stars....

Well priced charger but poor instructions and even my electrician said it was the worst most difficult set up and app he had ever known . App poor with lack of features. Tbh only the price and 5 year warranty is good .PS The company contacted me which was good , but the app not easy to use with too many options for charging and authorisations compared to previous chargers ive used.

The user pre-installation survey was a mammoth undertaking which required multiple photos of the electrical distribution box, Electric meter, Gas meter, Water pipe earth bonding etc. Something in the region of 9x photos and 1x video requested, and took a couple of hours to compile. The option to upload a video doesn't actually let you upload, only enables your camera.....That's fine if you're using a mobile phone, but I was using a laptop and had to carry the damn thing above my head to capture details from the laptop camera, and as the screen was pointing to the meters I could not see what I was recording.....Eventually I gave up. Installation was within the timeframe provided and was fairly seamless. The app, however, not so much. The app registration process asked for your house type, how many people live there, what type of car you have, who your service provider is etc and was unreasonably intrusive, especially considering that this takes place after the installation was already completed. I felt intimidated providing so much data just to get power to my charger. Once you have ran the gauntlet to get your app, there were two other issues: (1) adding a new user to share your account is not clearly indicated and took considerable research to share the charger with my partner. (2) Password, provided by the manufacturer, cannot be changed by the user. You have to call them to change a password. In conclusion, nice looking charger, fit for purpose, nicely installed. Very unimpressive app and way too many questions about my lifestile & choices just to get the damn app running. Would I recommend? Only if you can tolerate the questioning and are willing to jump through several hoops to get the end result. I'm happy with the product, but sit on the fence when it comes to recommending to others.Oh, and one final observation, I just looked at the website (via Halfords) and see that they have now increased the price by £50.00 and are offering a £50.00 discount marked "January sale". That's just nasty.
Works as described however the App is clunky and could use some more development.Support was great to help us get through the issues but the app still crashes occasionally.

I personally did not have a great experience with Cord EV. What I will say is that the issues I have are now sorted and towards the end of my experience, the help I did recieve, from Anthisham in particular, was fantastic. The product itself works perfectly fine and looks aesthetically pleasing. The app is also easy to use and integrates with my tariff well.

Liked: cheap, quick, nice people on the phoneDidn't like: had to be on the phone a lot, too many layers of subcontracting, not well enough regulated, installer failed to set up app to enable charging so had to DIY will telephone support, took 90 mins in the cold and wet. Cord androd app not working as wouldn't let me register the most basic details - postcode - so had to use another app

From start to finish my experience was dreadful.I ordered with cord due to these glowing reviews but my experience was far from that.I ordered using the government grant which was fine, a code was sent to me two days later after i had to keep chasing for it. I was told my charger would be installed within 2 weeks.I then enquired as to whether i needed surge or not, or if as i have a brand new build if this was already fitted. A week went past and no one replied to my email so i decided to call up, the girl i spoke to was actually helpful so i purchased this as an extra and was told this would arrive on the day of installation with the electrician.Not sure why they couldn't reply to my email in the first place?2 weeks went by and no mention of a date, i was advised i needed to call my electrical board in  my area as they had not yet had permission from the DNO, so i called them and they told me it was automatically accepted a week before as the usage of our house falls under the amount where if its below it is automatic approval.I called Cord and was told they had only had approval the same hour which was a blatant lie as the electric board even read the time and date out where the approval was granted.I was then contacted by a company that install on their behalf telling me it was going to be another 2 weeks, to which i said no way this is urgent and i was told 2 weeks nearly 3 weeks ago.I then contacted another company who could fit it the week after next.I decided to contact the company that contacted me to ask if there was any movement on install date so i could still use cord, and i was told no and if i want to use them there would be extra charges on top for ground work! This company had a copy of my home survery for 3 weeks and decided to tell me this far on that i was liable to have to pay out more money for groundworks? Why did they not tell me this 3 weeks before?This was the final nail in the coffin for me, not transparent and messed me arround massively with longer time scales and poor communication.I then did a return request through Cord, someone called me and i explained all this and they sympathised and promised a label would be sent to me so i could return to the post office and nothing arrived. A week later i chased and was told that the royal mail were collecting from my property that same day, lucky i was working from home on this day otherwise it would have failed. No one even bothered to tell me this was being collected.I can see one payment amount pending for a refund but the second amount i paid at a later date through PayPal for the surge device is yet to be refunded, and PayPal refunds are normally instant so now i have to chase this second refund.Not only has this taken up so much of my time for such a poor experience i am now having to chase my own refunds as they are incompetent at the whole sales process of taking money and refunding it when they let customers down.I ended up using Charge Point EV who installed within 5 working days, are great with communication and actually cheaper than Cord so use that information as you will.**Editing my review from 1 star to 3 stars, since leaving this the company director has reached out and apologised for the bad experience i had. He also sent me flowers, which in my opinion shows that Cord do care about the customer experience, but maybe their employees don't have that same level of customer service that James clearly posesses. I cannot give anymore than 3 stars as i cancelled my order and went elsewhere, but i wanted to edit my review to reflect what has happened since writing this. **

Ordering, delivery and booking fitting was good. Installation was carried out within the time scale advised  and was completed to a good standard. However - installer didn't tidy up very well. Brick dust from drilling through wall and for cable clips left on charger, charger cables and floor. Cable clippings left on floor in the garage. Biggest issue was I couldn't use the charger for two days due to software issue. On a positive note the customer support and tech team were very patient and helpful in getting the problem resolved. In use the charger works fine and I'm happy with it.

Great time and patience given from the customer service team. But initially the charger didn't work.I had a glitch with a faulty charger and its firmware, a factory fault not the supplier, Cord.To be absolutely fair, the customer service and efforts to rectify the matter were immediate(literally first thing next morning I received communication they would be back to sort the matter). Every possible way to rectify the issue was taken and the matter was sorted as a result.The charger works, and there was minimum disruption.No one's fault this end, but thoroughly accepting they were responsible for fixing the issue and they did.Thanks....

Please note edited review as issues have been addresed.After purchasing the home charger and completing the required home survey questionnaire there was around a 2 week delay with no contact so I chased this up. Lots of further questions asked from the installer AERO EV but eventually got a date for the installation. However the installer did not turn up on the planned date of install. The installer Danny from AERO absolutely fantastic and could't be any more pleased in how he did the installation. Only used the charger once so far but seems easy to use and the support team is available if there are any questions/issues.

The Cord subcontractor installers (Aero) were very poor with the pre assessment and the customer relations left much to be desired. Cord themselves were OKUPDATEFollowing my initial review Cord have been very proactive in following up on the issues I raised. They clearly value their reputation and more importantly their customers. I should give Richard in the Cord team a special mention as he supported me very well through the installation and issues at the time.

I liked that Cord EV is responsive - very quick to respond to queries and transparent.I disliked that it took longer than anticipated to get the EV charger installed with 3rd parties tasked with jobs such as survey not doing a comprehensive survey which led to delay and increase in cost. Also both suppliers given task to do groundworks and the installation cancelled on days they were booked to attend which affected our plans.Also, it would be great if Cord EV can help set client's expectation right from start as the website on buying the EV over promises with regards to the length of time the charger can be installed from purchase of the equipment.

The charger seems fine. The set up on my phone did not work.the guy who fitted the devise whilst very good at installing the charger was found wanting in terms of explaining how the charger will operate from my phone.

The EV charger starts drawing electricity 1.5 hours before the scheduled charge event. One of the founders of the company has taken personal ownership of the issue and I feel confident he will resolve it with excellent communication once I identified the issue.

The install had it's issues and some of the chargers abilities weren't utilised, however after contacting customer service this was rectified.The wifi to the device has caused some drop outs, and if the charger could accept an ethernet connection, it would solve these problems straight away, however that is not possible, so I've had to place a booster closer to the unit for it to have a stable connection.

Had to wait a little longer than the suggested 2 weeks however when the installation took place it was swift and easy the installer was professional and helpful took me through the APP to show me how it all worked have charged twice now with no problems.

We had a bit of a wait between taking delivery of the unit and a date for the fitting, possibly a time of year issue.The fitting engineer made a tidy job of installing our charger and getting us set up.All staff I have spoken with were very polite and helpfull, overall a good experience.

Cord offer a competitive solution to EV charging. I was then sent an authority to act form with no information on what it was or what to fill in on the form. again I contacted Cord who were helpful and provided this information.I then waited again to be contacted by the installers, I received a single phone call at 16:33 which I missed as I was leaving work. When I called back 1 minute later and the phone rang through to voicemail. 10 days later and many attempts to contact the installer, I have still not managed to speak to anyone from the installation company, I contacted Cord who kindly said they would try and even Cord have said they are having issues speaking to anyone. I am now on day 10 since the initial missed call from the installer with no confirmation of any dates for installation and still no contact with anyone who can install for me. I must assume that once contact is made, I will still be waiting weeks for an install date.While Cord appears great, I am starting to wish I purchased elsewhere, and even considering returning the charger if possible for a refund so I can go somewhere else.‐-----Update after the reply from cord:Yes, i had an email from the installer offering a provisional date of the 16th on the basis that i confirm this date by the end of the day. The email was sent to me at 16:09 on the 4th. I responded by email and tried to contact by phone them but was unsuccessful at speaking to anyone, nor did i have any confirmation that the provisional slot was held or confirmed. I’ve provisionally reserved Monday 16th for you — please let me know if this date works.I’ll hold this slot until close of play today.-----Since it appears nobody at Aero EV answers a phone or responds to any emails, i have to assume that slot was not held for me despite trying multiple times to contact them.Of course, i will contact Cord to talk to them as per their reply, and once again.. Changed instalation date twice from 1st date , failed to supply unit on the revised installation date , so half job done , this will mean it MIGHT be installed in February 2026 after placing the order in early december , where the halfords site i bought it through allowed me to pick at date for installation in December,  that was never going to happen.Since writing this review , things sprang into action , phone calls galore and fitted 2 days later . It could do with a feature in the app to restart the charging phase.Apart from that, the charger works fine and as it should. 5 stars if the fix the app issue when charging disrupted!
Had my cord EV charger installed today, thank you Tim,Excellent job

I would avoid this company.I had an EV charger installed by Octopus, there was an issue with the app showing an incorrect solar reading. Octopus told me to change the "CT clamp" to turn it around, if that didn't work they would send somebody out with an 8 week lead time, or have a local company fix it and they would cover the bill.I then rang EV heroes. They were brilliant, Suzanne was friendly and helpful. She arranged for somebody to come out in a couple of days.The engineer came and was brilliant too. He swapped the broken CT clamp as a contingency and said the issue was with the data going to the charger. He said it was a couple of hour job and the office would contact me.This is where it all went wrong. I started dealing with Maddie who wasn't helpful at all. She was hard to get hold of and didnt return my calls. When I did speak to her she told me that they dont usually do work where other companies have done the install - why on earth did they agree to come out then?! She also told me there was an issue with my solar panels and it would need a solar expert to come out.She continued to act slowly and I then received an excessive invoice of £460 for the work. This included the cost of 2 CT clamps, the callout fee and £200 for a solar expert to come.EV heroes then emailed me to say they had reviewed all the correspondence and felt they could no longer do any work for me. They told me as a goodwill gesture they would remove the cost of 1 CT clamp but insisted they had changed 2 to ensure they were the same style to prevent conflicts. The 2 different styles remain in my meter cupboard as plain as day.I informed them if they were going to tell me they wouldn't complete the job I wouldn't have agreed to instruct them and offered to pay for 1 CT clamp and £50 towards the initial visit.Maddie then said she would take me to court if I did not pay immediately and would go to Octopus to tell them the invoice was invalid - as if to suggest I was acting fraudulently and trying to get them to pay for work that didn't get done in the end.I then spoke to a solicitor and they advised me that EV Heroes had breached the contract. The invoice clearly stated the £150 fee was for a callout AND to "ensure CP Working correctly" which it still was not after they changed the CT clamp.I sent a pre-action letter and Maddie replied in a passive aggressive tone saying my communication style was poor and their customer service is excellent. It was quite surreal, the letter I wrote was professional and based on a template from the solicitor!Eventually Maddie said she would refund the callout fee, I still agreed to pay the cost of the CT clamp as it is not fair to receive money back for a physical part that they had installed.My advice therefore is to be very cautious about this callout fee. I assumed it places some expectation on them to complete the job - not to come out and tell me what work needs doing but then refusing to do anything further. They could go around being called out but never actually doing anything and receiving £150. Doesn't make sense does it.Also consider whether you want to deal with Maddie. She seemed to have a delusional viewpoint that her customer service was excellent but the reality is she would not accept that they got the CT clamp wrong, even with photographic proof. Offering to take it off as "goodwill" is not customer service - it is doing what they should have done at the start and apologised for. It seems she can't accept when she is wrong which is a huge problem if you work in customer service.Also for her to suggest she would contact Octopus is disgusting. It is a flagrant breach of GDPR and questions my integrity suggesting I would consider acting fraudulently.Please think long and hard about using EV Heroes. You'll see similar reviews and their passive aggressive replies on here when something goes wrong. The fact they refunded my money speaks volumes too - they can say whatever they like about this but if they believed they were right they would have defended their position at court.

No real apology or compensation for the issues from ev heroes or the installation company. EV heroes are no heroes. It has been a nightmare from start to finish today. a weak verbal apology and no compensation for the failure to complete the task today and they have been paid in full earlier in Jan 2026. I dont like to be forced to write such a poor review but it reflects what has actually occured.

Company were responsive, quick to install and tidied up afterwards.BUT the Evec charger that they recommended, or probably more specifically it's software, is awful.  It loses pairing and tells me it can't be found when I'm stood next to it with iPad or Phone.The charger and car port go 'Green' indicating charging, but half the time it's not actually charging when I think it is.When we move it can stay here and I'll get a proper unit from somewhere else.

Not a great experience in general due the lack of communication. Make sure you get everything in writing or email. No T&C to refer back to, no return policy if you're not happy with the product, which was imposed on me without consulting me about the brand of the charger.Taya who installed the charger was very pleasant, professional and polite. Work is not that easy when it was constantly raining but she did the job with no issue, thanks Taya.My issue was with the charger whic is not compatible with the Octopus intelligentgo plan which means that I can't do scheduled charging but instead, has to do this manually in the middle of the night. This is not convenient to me when I have work next day. When spoke to the customer service of the charger evec the lady mentioned that there are 'plans' to contact Octopus but not sure when.When asked to return or replace my charger, evheroes said that it's installed and used for two weeks now and there's no return policy if the charger is not faulty. It's not the cheapest also.Be careful before you buy from them.

We initially had the shell of our charger installed before the charging part due to unavailability. Although this shouldn't be an issue since the car delivery is not imminent, it turned out that the wires used for the charger circuit were too small for the car. This was pointed out by the meter engineer during the smart meter installation.However, I must acknowledge and commend the diligent engineer who went above and beyond to ensure the safety of our installation. After installing the body of the charger, the company's own engineer rechecked and found out that the entire circuit had been suboptimally installed, which required a complete undo and reinstall process, making a 30-minute job take 3.5 hours. I appreciate the engineer's proactive efforts in ensuring our installation's safety.The company has been in touch with me several times to rectify the situation and requested me to remove my review. I expressed my requirement for a safety certificate to replace the previous one from Siemens, which had deemed the installation dangerous, to ensure my peace of mind. The company claimed to have checked with Siemens and reissued my safety certificate before their engineer reinstalled the circuit.Relevant dates:- original install 4/1/23- Siemens engineer changed meters and informed us the charger circuit was dangerous 17/3/23- new safety certificate for the installation provided by EVHeroes 5/4/23- installation reinstalled by EVHeroes engineer 6/4/23

There were seemingly endless requests for photos and information from ev heroes I would have much preferred a site visit to sort out any issues. The charger they supplied has a 5 metre cable I requested a 7.5m or 10m charging cable but was told this was not available. All in all a poor experience would not recommend

Sales team very helpful, discovered a minor issue with instalation. Didn't bother bringing this to their attention, drilled right through single skin, brick built garage wall for the entire cable run. Sorted the software problems direct with the charger manufacturer

I spent a while talking to the sales team before making my decision to purchase. It was between EV Heroes and Octopus for purchase and installation. What swung it for me for EV Heroes was that they told me they do not use 3rd party installers - it's all 'in house' so if a problem occurs, it's an easy fix.This turned out not to be true.The sales person also talked me out of the Zappi as 'old technology' and tried to sell me something that wasn't compatible with Octopus Intelligent Go. So, I decided to go with a Hypervolt Home Pro 3 as this was definitely compatible according to the Octopus website.The install day came. The person that arrived was a 3rd party contractor - when I queried this with him, he said that 'some' of them are employed directly by EV Heroes, but not him. He didn't do the best job - left a mess and damaged the handle of the charger. I had to call EV Heroes to replace it, as well as order a 2nd CT clip as we have solar / battery (which the sales rep knew about) because EV Heroes hadn't ordered one. I also waited over 4 weeks and chased twice to get someone to come and fix the charger handle and fit the CT. The person that arrived agreed that the first engineer hadn't done the job particularly well.I have also since discovered that the Zappi charger has better integration with the Octopus app than the Hypervolt, so I was disappointed to discover this when my process for charging was something of a faff compared to my neighbour with her Octopus installed Zappi.They got the job done to a satisfactory level eventually, but it was a slog. I also felt cheated out of a better option (the Zappi) despite being very clear on my requirements when I made my initial enquiries with supposed experts.

The unit I chose for installation turned out almost immediate to be a terrible piece of kit that did not work (Ohme). Not EV Heroes' fault and they did eventually agree to replace with a different unit at no extra cost. But it was disappointing that when I raised the problem with the first unit EVH told me to discuss with the manufacturer, which took nearly 2 months until the manufacturer agreed the unit was a dud. Would have expected more helpful service given their role as the vendor and installer, the size of purchase and that like I assume many EV charger purchasers this was my first experience with the tech.Friendly and professional staff when fitting the unit and price charged was very competitive.
Very slow response to begin work; clearly approval was delayed somehow, suggesting a breakdown in communication.

Good quick service for installation.Unfortunately my charging unit failed on the first usage. I had three further call outs one for assessment and another two for replacement of the unit and then the circuit breaker switch.I think I was just unlucky but overall a good experience with communication and happy with the unit I selected and the app and advice I received.

The process from start to finish was very easy and someone (Rachel) was always at the end of the phone to help with any enquiries and she looked after us through the whole process.I would suggest that if as in our case, your install isn't looking straight forward that EV Heroes offer a service to send out their own engineer to assess as they are the professionals and can gauge what is required as we had a bit of to and fro regarding the way we could have ours fitted and wired.We initially had a date booked before Christmas and a sub contractor was used who didn't turn out at all helpful.  I was put off by this and genuinely thought... Oh no.  In the end the install didn't happen that day but I thought I'd give the helpful Rachel a call.She was great and after I'd asked for one of their own engineers to attend this was arranged and booked in for early January.Kyle messaged me with an ETA that morning which was a great start.  He then turned up and was very professional.  I showed him where everything was and Kyle decided that the install could be a much simpler affair and put his skills to work by doing some tests on our existing electric supply to our garage resulting in significantly less work and costs on both sides.Throughout the morning he explained everything that was going on, from our supply to safety to the set up of our charger and app and ensured everything was tidied up and working properly before leaving.  He was brilliant.  An absolute credit to EV Heroes.  He even followed up on a Saturday asking if everything was ok and working properly.  Superb service.Once this was done, we were forwarded the remaining invoice.  I had a query with the change to the works needed which was reflected in the invoice.  Rachel explained the changes and handled the payment.  Very easy to get hold of again and would recommend.The only reason I can't give 5 stars is due to the subcontractor and how it would have been easier throughout if one of their engineers had popped in and assessed prior.
Work carried and discussed as promised.Efficient and clean with work.One star down for only sending electrical safety certificate 4 months after completion.
Good company to deal with polite reasonable informative helpful prompt, happy to sort things, what more could you ask for?

The installation was carried out after a 4 week wait, due to high demand.  The installer arrived promptly at 8.00 am and the installation was completed by 11.30 am.The only reason I have dropped a single star is due to the mess that was left outside (dust, mastic residue, dislodged pebble dash etc)  He cleaned up inside but left the outside!
Very good and friendly staff good service

The charger was fitted very well, and the engineer was polite and professional, however, the charger is still only putting out 2.2kwh and not the 7.4 its supposed to do.I am a little frustrated as it takes over 12 hours to charge the car up to 50%
This was because technician was good but we were not made aware that the app can only be run by one person so only the main driver can turn on or off the wall box
So far very good. The downside is sending all the photos of electricity meters and gas - especially when like mine, they are in the most awkward place.

Overall experience was good and would use again. Only negative was the amount of info they wanted to claim the grant. Some you would expect such as photos of the meter box and distribution board but when l was asked for pics of earth bonding to the gas meter and a floor plan of the house I was ready to cancel. I had already agreed the wallbox was going right beside the outside meter box.The installation was as planned and very tidy

Using EV Heroes was a good experience.  Rabecca who answered the phone and took me through my order was excellent both efficient and professional. Ellis who came and fitted the EV was helpful, polite and kept me fully informated when he woiuld arrie, how long the job would take etc he also walked me through the installation, loading the app etc.
Had our charge point fitted last year and received fabulous service from the installer and all staff in the office. Quick, easy process and would highly recommend to anyone.
Excellent service from start to finish engineer clean and tidy and explained everything would highly recommend.
Great service from the initial phone call to the install date.Installer was very professional and tidy.Thanks EvheroesPaul

EV Heroes and especially Conor that came to our house today was a true professional. He took great care in ensuring the charger was installed with the least amount of internal cable on show as possible and took great care as if it was his own home. He was courteous, polite and tidied up everything before he left. A true credit to EV Heroes! Well done 👏
I highly recommend EV Hero for EV charger installation. Rebecca Whiddon was extremely helpful throughout the entire process, and I am very satisfied with the service.
Very prompt and friendly service. Installed our charger within 2 weeks, they were happy to answer any questions beforehand and helped us to apply for the grant.
Absolutely fantastic experience and excellent service, 100% recommend! Right from initial contact to installation, everything went smooth and was sorted as promised.

Can not recommend this company highly enough, I never normally write reviews however had to as the customer service was arguably the best I’ve ever received.Call centre , knew there stuff technical and booked an appointment in straight away , prompt responses on WhatsApp when I had few questions pre install . Engineer turn up exactly on time ,  explained everything workmanship excellent tidy & neat install no rubbish left , gave demo on app.This company has definitely got it right . Great Ethos running throughout the business Will recommend to everyone I know .

Not the easiest of installs to do due to the size and complexity of the building but install is now in and all working as it should.Maddison helped to overcome some issues we ran into and accommodated our limited availability to be home at times .
Paul was great! Helpful and efficient - communicated with EV Charger company to get box active and got us set up.
Very easy to deal with, very professional

Absolutely amazing to deal with. Nothing was to much trouble. No pushing to commit  to install a charger, quick responses on questions, quick installation when I decided. just a friendly experience from start to finish. Brilliant!!

Customer oriented approach, not just about the profit. From the customer service desk all the way through to the installation engineer. Professional, efficient and quick service - can absolutely recommend

I had my EV charger fitted to a pedestal and all went smoothly they did a very good job. A few weeks later it stopped working so I called them. No problem they arranged for a electrician to attend and he fixed the problem in a few minutes. A good service and I am very happy. Highly recommend them
Job well done in the times promised.Very understanding and helpful company
Installation was arranged quickly and carried out without any problems.  Using the charger is easy. I would definitely recommend this company.
Brilliant customer service from start to finish and after care. Really knowledgeable team on the products available. After care has been outstanding. Highly responsive, would definitely recommend.
